Summary: Unable to open?Hardware Setup Wizard for?HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices after installation from Add-on Manager
Description: When you download and install?HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices  from Add-on Manager  and click?Setup Now  option, Hardware Setup Wizard may throw following error:? ? Caught "std::exception" Exception message is:?  Interrupted. ?Parsing XML file ?
Workaround: To avoid this issue, you can use these workarounds:Workaround 1: Once HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices?are installed, close the current MATLAB session and re-open MATLAB. Then through?Add-Ons?->?Manage Add-Ons,?open Hardware Setup Wizard?to setup HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices.Workaround 2: Use this workaround if you are unable to resolve the issue using workaround 1.?Download and install latest R2024a MATLAB.Download support package installer file for HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices?from?Link.Run support package installer file with R2024a MATLAB.Once support package is installed,?open Hardware Setup Wizard for HDL Coder Support Package for Xilinx FPGA and SoC Devices through?Add-Ons?->?Manage Add-Ons.

Summary: Unable to use MATLAB HDF4 import/export functionality on Apple Silicon machines
Description: Using the MATLAB high- or low-level functions for importing or exporting HDF4 or HDF-EOS data on Apple Silicon machines can result in workflow issues, including loss of data and HDF4 library errors.

Summary: Programming language reserved identifier?error when same?char alias used for data type replacement?and for block or signal output
Description: Suppose you use?Simulink.AliasType ?to create an alias for int8? or?uint8? named?MyCharType ? and these conditions apply: The alias is used as a replacement name for char  in the?Code Generation > Data Type Replacement ?pane. For example, running the command?get_param(modelName, 'ReplacementTypes').char? returns?'MyCharType' .  The alias is used to specify the data type of a block output or signal in the model. For example, running the command?get_param(blockPath, 'OutDataTypeStr') ?returns?'MyCharType' . ?  ?When you generate code for the model, you might see an error like this: Unable to generate code using user-defined data type 'MyCharType' because it conflicts with a programming language reserved identifier?

Summary: Code generated from models using Specialized Power Systems blocks might crash
Description: The code that you generate from models that use Specialized Power Systems blocks might crash. This issue occurs only during the execution of code from discrete simulation of certain models that contain switches and non-linear elements.  These crashes are limited to the execution of the generated code and do not impact the standard desktop simulations.

Summary: Code generator might throw error for atomic subsystem when block parameter?Schedule as?is set to?Periodic partition?or?Aperiodic partition
Description: The code generator might throw an error when generating code for an atomic subsystem when either of these combinations of conditions exist: Subsystem block parameter?Schedule as? is set to?Periodic partition ?or? Aperiodic partition ?and? block parameter? Function packaging? is set to?Nonreusable function ?or? Reusable function . Subsystem is inside an atomic subsystem or a referenced model and subsystem block parameter?Schedule as? is set to?Periodic partition ?or? Aperiodic partition.   For the first case, the error message looks like this: For Subsystem block <blockPath>, block parameter 'Schedule as' is set to Periodic partition and 'Function packaging' is set to 'Reusable' or 'Nonreusable'. To generate code, set 'Schedule as' to 'Sample time' or set 'Function packaging' to 'Auto' or 'Inline'.   For the second case, the error message looks like this: Subsystem block <blockPath> is inside atomic subsystem <parentBlockPath or referencedModel> and has block parameter 'Schedule as' set to Periodic partition. To generate code, move Subsystem block out of atomic subsystem or set 'Schedule as' to 'Sample time'.
Workaround: To avoid the error conditions, update the model as the error messages suggest.

Summary: Search & Replace in state transition tables or truth tables might not correctly replace variable names
Description: When using the Search & Replace function, variable names might not be correctly replaced if searching within astate transition table or truth table.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when simulating Stateflow chart that uses C as the action language and includes a cast operator
Description: When updating or simulating a model that contains a Stateflow chart that uses C as the action language, MATLAB crashes if the chart contains a cast operator with fixed-point type or 64-bit integer type of the below form: cast(data, sfix8_en2)?  or? cast(data, int64)?
Workaround: For cast operator with fixed-point types, use the fixdt function:?cast(data, fixdt(1, 8, 2)) instead of cast(data, sfix8_en2)?For cast operator with 64-bit integer types, use C-style cast operator:int64(data) instead of cast(data, int64)?

Summary: Missing reference lines and filled regions in yyaxis plots
Description: Reference lines (created with xline  or yline ) and filled regions (created with xregion  or yregion ) might not appear in yyaxis  plots in the Live Editor or when loading a saved FIG-file.

Summary: chunkDataInfo output table has incorrect variable name: Chunk Data
Description: The chunkDataInfo  function output table includes a variable incorrectly named Chunk Data . This variable should be named ChunkData .

Summary: Code generator might throw error for an atomic subsystem that uses virtual bus input when block parameter Schedule as is set to Periodic partition or Aperiodic partition
Description: ?The code generator might throw an error when generating code for an atomic subsystem when these conditions exist: Subsystem uses virtual bus input. Subsystem block parameter Schedule as  is set to Periodic partition  or? Aperiodic partition .   The error message looks like this: Subsystem block <blockPath> is using virtual bus at port 1 and block parameter 'Schedule as' is set to Periodic partition. To generate code, change virtual bus to nonvirtual bus or set 'Schedule as' to 'Sample time'.
Workaround: To avoid the error condition, update the model as the error message suggests.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when the GPU Coder App uses Check for Run-Time Issues?for a deep learning network that contains convolution layers
Description: MATLAB crashes when using GPU Coder App if?both ?conditions are true: The CPU  option is selected in the Check for Run-Time Issues  dialog box. The deep learning network used for code generation contains convolution layers.?

Summary: Half-Bridge (Ideal, Switching) block incorrectly uses Initial node temperatures, [T1,T2,...,Tn] parameter for diode Cauer thermal networks
Description: This issue occurs if the parameters of the Half-Bridge (Ideal, Switching) block in your model satisfy all ?of these conditions:  The Integral protection diode  parameter is set to Yes  The Thermal network  parameter is set to Cauer model parameterized with Foster coefficients  Both the Separate thermal ports for upper and lower devices  and Separate thermal ports for integral diodes  parameters are selected   In such situations, the diode thermal networks incorrectly use the?Initial node temperatures, [T1,T2,...,Tn] ?parameter instead of using the?Diode initial node temperatures, [T1,T2,...,Tn] ?parameter.

Summary: Compatibility check fails when the model contains a Function Caller block which calls a Simulink Function having bus arguments.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails when:The model contains a Function Caller  block which calls a Simulink Function  having buses as the input or output arguments.  The Simulink Function  is not defined in the model that contains the Function Caller  block.  Sample error message:  Failed to compile <model name>

Summary: Intel Hard Floating Point Mapping?Failure for?Data Type Conversion (DTC) block with integer ports.
Description: Under certain conditions, HDL Coder may fail to generate HDL code for designs that incorporate a Data Type Conversion (DTC) block. These conditions include:  Use Intel Quartus Pro as the synthesis tool, Intel AlteraFPFunctions vendor library is enabled in the floating point configuration, and The DTC block has ports with integer data type with a word length either less than 16 bits or more than 128 bits.   In such scenarios, HDL Coder could fail to produce HDL code, displaying an error message that indicates: No matching files named ' /<path_to_design>/Altera/<Device_Family>/<Part #>/alterafpf_fixed_to_floating_numerictype_to_single_fixpt_s1_w0_f0 to single/synth' were not found.?
Workaround: This issue can be avoided by utilizing the Quartus II synthesis tool.

Summary: Inherited data error when mapping an array element to Data Store Memory in an atomic subchart
Description: Stateflow might throw a misleading error when you try to update or run a model if all of the following conditions are true: A Stateflow chart uses a Data Store Memory (DSM) The DSM is defined in Simulink to store an array The DSM data in chart has the same name as the DSM in Simulink The chart has an atomic subchart with a DSM data mapped to a single element of the DSM array  The error thrown is similar to: Atomic subchart accesses main chart data store memory data with inherited size,  type,  complexity via mapped data store memory data.  Subchart data store memory data can only map to main chart data if its size,  type,  and complexity are fully specified.?  The error should state that mapping a single element of a DSM array is not supported.

Summary: Diode block incorrectly calculates charge dynamics when using peak reverse current and reverse recovery charge
Description: In the Diode block, if you set the Charge dynamics  parameter to Use peak reverse current and reverse recovery charge , the software incorrectly calculates the charge dynamics due to an incorrect implementation of the reverse recovery charge equation.  The block currently calculates the reverse recovery charge, Qrr , by using this equation: Qrr = -iRM^2/2a + tau_rr*iRM?   where iRM  is the peak reverse current, a  is the rate of change of current when measuring iRM , and tau_rr  is the reverse recovery time constant. However, this is the correct equation: Qrr = -iRM^2/2a - tau_rr*iRM ?
Workaround: Set the Charge dynamics parameter to Use peak reverse current and reverse recovery time. This option enables the Reverse recovery time, trr parameter. To calculate the value of this parameter, use these equations:tau_rr = -iRM/2a - Q_rr/iRM?trr = tau_rr*log(10)+iRM/awhere?iRM?is the peak reverse current,?Qrr?is the reverse recovery charge, and?a is the rate of change of current when measuring iRM.

Summary: Diode block does not model charge dynamics when modeling tabulated diodes
Description: If, in the Diode block, you set the Diode model  parameter to Tabulated I-V curve , the block does not model charge dynamics independently of the value you specify for the Charge dynamics  parameter. The simulation of your model with a Diode block with these settings might yield inaccurate results.

Summary: Code generated for a model that contains a MATLAB System block might contain multiple definitions of an enumerated type
Description: When a model contains a MATLAB System block that references data of an enumerated type, the code generator might generate multiple definitions of that enumerated type. This results in uncompilable code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parameter Writer blocks that write to different model workspace variables in same type of subsystems generate incorrect code
Description: Incorrect code generation occurs for Parameter Writer blocks when all the following conditions are met: Parameter Writer blocks write to different model workspace variables. The Parameter Writer blocks are in same type of subsystems. For example, the Parameter Writer blocks are in different function-call subsystems.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: ?On transitions in charts that use C as the action language, count and duration operators might return incorrect value
Description: Values returned by the count  and duration  operators might be incorrect if all of the following conditions are true: The chart uses C action language The operators are used in a condition, condition action, or transition action of a transition The argument to the count  or duration  operator is changed elsewhere within the chart by unary operators  For example, if a state had the following during action:? during:??x++;?y = count(x>0);  and an outgoing transition:? E{z = count(x>0);}  the value of y  and z would not match as expected.?

Summary: Using nonscalar query points in table lookup may lead to wrong answer
Description: When a model contains blocks that use table lookup, it can generate a wrong answer if both of these conditions are true: A table lookup is evaluated for a nonscalar query point. The model has a high differential index and requires index reduction.
Workaround: When authoring a custom Simscape block, you can use a set of table lookup calls, each evaluated for a scalar query point, instead of using a single table lookup call for a nonscalar query point.

Summary: Importing FMU 3.0 with boolean array parameters causes type mismatch error in Simulink
Description: An unexpected error occurs when an FMU version 3.0 block with boolean array parameters is imported into Simulink.? The following error message is generated when the model is updated or simulated: 'Scalar type does not match the FMU model variable <parameter name> defined in the modelDescription.xml file. Cast 'Boolean' values to 'boolean'.'  This error message incorrectly implies that there is a scalar type mismatch despite the parameters being correctly defined in the modelDescription.xml file.?

Summary: System Composer report generation error: Unrecognized function or variable 'row'
Description: When using programmatic interfaces to generate a report on a System Composer model, you might encounter the error: Unrecognized function or variable 'row'

Summary: Accelerated simulation fails for a model hierarchy containing reference models with model arguments
Description: Accelerated simulation of a model hierarchy containing a referenced model with model arguments can fail if the referenced model has the model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model ?set to Multiple? and a referenced model higher in the hierarchy has the model configuration parameter?Total number of instances allowed per top model ?set to?One.? In this case, when you compile the simulation target code for the referenced model higher in the hierarchy, a memcpy is executed on an undeclared identifier and Simulink reports an error similar to:? ?mMid_msf.c:250:40: error: 'fzidmdwontw' undeclared (first use in this function) ?{ return rtS ; } ( void ) memcpy ( & ( fzidmdwontw . fdvovoslul . rtm .
Workaround: Set the model configuration parameter Total number of instances allowed per top model?to Multiple for all referenced models in the hierarchy.

Summary: Simulink might hang during model update phase of model containing Simulink Function block
Description: Simulink might hang during model update phase of model under the following conditions:?Model block references an export-function model containing a Simulink Function block. The Simulink Function block is either: Called by a Function Caller block inside an Initialize Function block within the same model; or Global or port-scoped. The?Function visibility ?parameter of the Trigger block inside of the Simulink Function block is set to global  or port .  The Simulink Function block contains a Ground block connected to a Goto block and the Goto block has a corresponding From block.
Workaround: Replace the Ground block with a Constant block with value 0;?or insert a Mux block with an input port between the Ground block and the Goto block.

Summary: Loading or navigating into a Simulink model linked from a System Composer component might corrupt parameter values defined in the Model Workspace
Description: Parameters defined in the Model Workspace might get corrupted when the parameters have a data type that is not a double, single, integer or logical and either of following conditions are true:? You load or navigate into a Simulink model that is linked to a component in System Composer. The value of a parameter is edited from the linked component.
Workaround: Consider either of the following workarounds:Load the Simulink model linked to the component before loading or opening the System Composer model.Uncheck the Argument checkbox for parameters with types other than double, single, integer or logical.?

Summary: Generated model might be incorrect when you use the Tapped Delay Enabled Synchronous block parallel to the HDL FIFO block.??
Description: Incorrect model generated after HDL code generation when the following conditions are true:? Model contains the?HDL FIFO? block parallel to the?Tapped Delay Enabled Synchronous ?block. Distributed Pipelining  option in Configuration Parameter >HDL Code Generation > Optimizations ?is enabled.?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when root outputs are updated for non-default cases of Switch Case blocks
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code for a model that meets these conditions: The model contains a Switch Case block that has the?Show default case  parameter check box selected. The Switch Case block has two output cases: one default and one non-default. The output signals of the Switch Case block feed into action subsystems. The output signals of the action subsystems are connected to different blocks. The model configuration parameter Eliminate superfluous local variables (Expression?folding)  is?enabled.
Workaround: Add a second non-default output caseto the Switch Case block. The output of the second non-default output case canbe left terminated.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: User-specified globalfimath?properties are ignored when using cast like to convert a non-fi object to a fi object in interpreted execution
Description: In interpreted execution, when you use cast ?to convert ?a non-fi  object to a fi  object MATLAB ignores any previously user-specified globalfimath  settings. In code generation, MATLAB follows the globalfimath  properties. This might cause a discrepancy between interpreted execution and code generation. For example, for this MATLAB code: globalfimath('RoundingMethod','Floor')?  cast(0.75,'like',fi([],1,8,0))?  The interpreted execution returns an incorrect value of 1 and the code generation returns a value of 0.

Summary: Unexpected gray (unreachable) code in main generator mode in presence of non-finite float variables
Description: If you explicitly initialize global variables to NaN  in your code and your code does not contain a main()  function, you might see most functions in your code as unreachable despite using the option -allow-non-finite-floats .

Summary: Rapid accelerator simulations and simulations deployed using Simulink Compiler error if the generated executable file is moved
Description: Rapid accelerator simulations with the up-to-date check disabled and simulations deployed using Simulink Compiler issue an error if both of these conditions are met:? The simulation tunes one or more variables whose values are structures. The executable generated for the rapid accelerator or deployed simulation has been moved from the build-time location.  For example, suppose the executable for a rapid accelerator simulation of the model mdl  is built in the directory C:\buildtime\  and then you move the executable to a different location, such as C:\runtime . A rapid accelerator simulation of the model mdl ?that has the up-to-date check disabled and tunes one or more structure variables issues an error similar to this one:  Unable to construct a MAT-file based variable evaluator: the MAT-file 'C:\buildtime\slprj\raccel\model\model_default_variable_values.mat' was not found. ?

Summary: FastText English 16 Billion Token Word Embedding support package silently fails to install on Apple Silicon
Description: On Apple Silicon Mac machines, the?fastTextWordEmbedding ?function throws an unexpected error. If you install the Text Analytics Toolbox Model?for fastText English 16 Billion Token Word Embedding? support package and call fastTextWordEmbedding , then the function displays an error message indicating the support package is not installed. >> fastTextWordEmbedding  Error using fastTextWordEmbeddingfastTextWordEmbedding requires the Text Analytics Toolbox Model for fastText English 16 Billion Token Word Embedding?support package. To install this support package, use the Add-On Explorer.
Workaround: Download the fastText English 16 Billion Token Word Embedding file ("wiki-news-300d-1M.vec.zip") to your working directory from?https://fasttext.cc/docs/en/english-vectors.html.?To load the data into MATLAB,?use the wordEmbedding function.>> emb = wordEmbedding("wiki-news-300d-1M.vec.zip");

Summary: Genomics Viewer app fails to open
Description: Starting in January 2024, Genomics Viewer is no longer able to load the default reference genome (HG38), causing the app to not load.

Summary: pearspdf and pearscdf return incorrect values for the PDF and CDF, respectively, for inputs corresponding to a Pearson type 4 distribution
Description: pearspdf  and pearscdf  return incorrect values for the PDF and CDF, respectively, for inputs corresponding to a Pearson type 4 distribution. The Pearson type 4 distribution occurs when certain criteria are met based on the skew  and kurt  inputs to pearspdf  and pearscdf . To determine what Pearson type a given skew  and kurt  correspond to, you can use the type output from pearspdf  and pearscdf : >> mu = 1;>> sigma = 1;>> skew = 1;>> kurt = 10;>> [~, type] = pearspdf([],mu,sigma,skew,kurt) All other Pearson types computed by pearspdf  and pearscdf  are unaffected and return the correct values.
Workaround: Use the attached file, convertFromSigmaToScalePearsonIV, to transform the desired sigma input into one that pearspdf and pearscdf can use. This transformation results in a different sigma value that is passed in to pearspdf and pearscdf, but the output from pearspdf and pearscdf corresponds to a Pearson type 4 distribution with a sigma value as originally desired (not the transformed value). Note that this function only modifies sigma if the inputs correspond to a Pearson type 4 distribution, so it is safe to call it on a sigma input to pearspdf and pearscdf even if you don't know what Pearson type your inputs correspond to:>> mu = 1;>> sigma = 1;>> skew = 1;>> kurt = 10;>> x = 0:.01:10;% Convert sigma. The modified sigma passed to pearspdf will return a PDF corresponding to a Pearson type 4 distribution with sigma=1 as desired>> sigma = convertFromSigmaToScalePearsonIV(mu,sigma,skew,kurt);>> f = pearspdf(x,mu,sigma,skew,kurt);

Summary: Non-Direct Feedthrough Flag is not honored for bus type input ports in S-Functions generated with S-Function Builder Block?
Description: In S-Function Builder, bus ports are always Direct Feedthrough, even if you uncheck the Direct Feedthrough  option on the settings pane.

Summary: Longitudinal Vehicle block errors when the road slope port?is hidden
Description: Simulating a model that contains a Longitudinal Vehicle block generates the error message "Number of variables exceeds number of equations " when the?Show road slope inport ?parameter is cleared.
Workaround: To maintain a constant zero slope, select?Show road slope inport, and leave port PG?or port NG?disconnected.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2024a Update 1

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur for Assignment blocks use?Starting index (dialog)?as the?Index Option
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code for a model that meets these conditions:? The model contains an Assignment block?whose Index?Option ?parameter is set to?Starting index (dialog) .  The signal entering the input data port (U ) of the Assignment block is connected to the output signal of another Assignment block or a For Each subsystem.
Workaround: Clear the?Perform in-place updates for Assignment and Bus Assignment blocks?configuration parameter checkbox.

Summary: Code generation might fail if?Scale?or?Bias?propertyof a scaling layer?is a row vector
Description: Code generation might fail if both  these?conditions are true:?  Your MATLAB code contains a network that has a ScalingLayer .   The?Scale ?or?Bias ?property of this scaling layer is a row vector.  Forexample,?code generation might fail for this network:? scale = [1 2 3];bias = scale;dlnet = dlnetwork([imageInputLayer([2 3 4]); scalingLayer(Scale=scale,Bias=bias)]);
Workaround: Manually expand the row vector Scale or Bias property along at least one other dimension so that this property is no longer a row vector.For example, you can expand Scale along the height dimension, while specifying the same numeric scale and bias values to the layer:scale = [1 2 3];expandedScale = repmat(scale, [2 1]);expandedBias = expandedScale;dlnet = dlnetwork([imageInputLayer([2 3 4]); scalingLayer(Scale=expandedScale,Bias=expandedBias)]);

Summary: importhdl?function may fail to execute when processing a large number of input Verilog files.?
Description: When you input a large number of Verilog files to the?importhdl  function, importhdl  may fail to process the Verilog files and may be unable to generate Simulink model. The following error message is thrown:? ?"Unable to create the slx file, because the specified folder is read only".

Summary: Error when simulating models containing a Model block with Show model initialize port enabled
Description: If a referenced model contains a port-scoped Simulink Function block, Simulink might incorrectly report the following error during simulation. Unable to register events from referenced model when showing initialize, reset or terminate ports on model block? ?  Simulink will report this error when all the following conditions are true: Simulink Function block Scope ?parameter is set to port . The model containing the Simulink Function block is referenced in another model with a Model block. The Model block Show model initialize port ?parameter is enabled.

Summary: ForEach Subsystem configured with Mask Parameter might result in internal assertion.
Description: HDL Coder runs into the assertion shown below if the mask parameter undergoing partitioning in the ForEach subsystem has a WordLength > 32?? Internal Error: Evaluation of validate function at block path 'mre/hdl_mre/For Each Subsystem/For Each' failed with the error message: Unexpected error during HDL Code Generation. Contact support@mathworks.com with reproduction steps. Assertion failed with debug message: matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_tags\ForEachDataTag.hpp:199:nativeVObj.get() Expected?conformance message "Generate parameterized HDL code from masked subsystem' is not supported when the word length of the mask?parameter 'x' is greater than 32 bit"  ?

Summary: Simulation result is incorrect when a model contains a Parameter Writer block that writes to bus type parameter and simulates in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Model with a Parameter Writer block produces incorrect results in rapid accelerator mode?when all the following conditions occur: The Parameter Writer block is inside an Initialize Function block.?  The Parameter Writer block writes to a bus type model workspace parameter, mask parameter, or block parameter.

Summary: RoadRunner crashes if a scene file containing multiple aerial images is merged into an existing scene file
Description: RoadRunner crashes if a scene file containing multiple aerial images is merged into an existing scene file.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated from a multi-rate model reference containing an initialize or terminate subsystem might be incorrect
Description: Incorrect code generation occurs under the following conditions: Top model contains an atomic subsystem That atomic subsystem contains a model reference block The referenced model contains an initialize or terminate subsystem The referenced model has multiple execution rates  Under these conditions, the initialize function for the top model might incorrectly contain a call to the atomic subsystem output function associated with the base rate of the top model?(Output0 or Step0).?  As an example of this issue, the following initialize   function is for a top model that has an atomic subsystem called?myAtomicSubsystem, which contains the referenced model. The initialize  function incorrectly contains the myAtomicSubsystemTID0()  call:?? void initialize(void)?? {???? ReferencedModel_initialize(rtmGetErrorStatusPointer(rtM));???? myAtomicSubsystem_Init();???? myAtomicSubsystemTID0();?? }

Summary: Model Testing Dashboard and metrics engine API may return warning about deleted results for coverage metrics
Description: The Model Testing Dashboard and metrics engine API may return the following message when collecting metric results for the Requirements-Based Tests and Unit-Boundary Tests coverage metrics: Warning: Result has been deleted.  In the Model Testing Dashboard, the message appears as an error in the Diagnostics  panel. If you collect metric results using the metric engine API, the message appears as a warning in the MATLAB Command Window. The message appears when the metrics engine does not load test results in the correct order.
Workaround: Use one of the following:Export all test results for your unit into a single file.Manually import the test results for your unit into Simulink Test.

Summary: Incorrect FPGA programming for AXI4-Stream reference design on Xilinx?Versal?AI Core Series?VCK190?Evaluation Kit
Description: When using the??"Default system with AXI4-Stream interface" reference design with the Xilinx?Versal?AI Core Series?VCK190?Evaluation Kit, the FPGA programming does not correctly program the board. In particular, the programming operation will: Correctly download the bitstream to the FPGA Incorrectly set the devicetree on the board  This is observed from all of the following programming workflows: Running the Program Target Device task in the HDL Workflow Advisor Clicking the Program Target Device button on the HDL Code toolstrip Running the?programFPGA ?function on the xilinxsoc object?  This can affect workflows that interact with the design running on the FPGA, such as: Prototyping the design using the host interface script. The generated host interface script may error out with the message "Failedto find device: mwipcore0:mm2s0 " Running the software interface model on the ARM processor
Workaround: After programming the FPGA using one of the workflows described above, you can manually set the correct devicetree by running the follow commands in MATLAB:?% (1) Connect to your hardware boardhProcessor = xilinxsoc;% (2) Backup the existing devicetree?system(hProcessor, 'cp /mnt/system.dtb /mnt/system_backup.dtb');?% (3) Set the correct devicetree for the "Default system with AXI4-Stream interface" reference designsystem(hProcessor, 'cp /mnt/dtbs/devicetree_axistream_iio.dtb /mnt/system.dtb');% (4) Reboot the board?system(hProcessor, 'systemctl reboot');Note: If you manually set the devicetree in this manner, and later want to reprogram the board with a non-AXI4-Stream design, you must restore the original devicetree BEFORE reprogramming. You can use this command to restore the original devicetree:?system(hProcessor, 'cp /mnt/system_backup.dtb /mnt/system.dtb');If you do not restore the original devicetree, the board may fail to boot properly. In such a case where the board fails to boot, you can return to the Guided Hardware Setup and follow the steps to reflash your SD card, which will restore the original devicetree.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code is missing for a Hit Crossing block in rapid accelerator mode
Description: The function-call code in the generated code for a Hit Crossing block is missing when all the following conditions occur:? Output type ?parameter?of the Hit Crossing block is set to Function-Call  signal.? The Hit Crossing block is driven by a block with constant sample time in rapid accelerator mode.
Workaround: Set a non-constant sample?time for the?block that drives the Hit Crossing block.

Summary: Spectrum Analyzer with dBFS SpectrumUnits might crash MATLAB
Description: Spectrum Analyzer block can cause MATLAB(R) to crash if these conditions are met: 1) Spectrum Unit in the Spectrum tab is set to dBFS. 2) Input signal is a complex scalar.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generic C/C++ code for a network that contains scaling layers
Description: Generated generic C/C++ code for networks with a rl.layer.ScalingLayer  might return incorrect results or produce an error during execution if all  the following conditions are true:? In the dee learning configuration object,? TargetLibrary  is set to 'none' .  The network has a scaling layer. Both the Scale  and Bias  properties of the scaling layer are nonscalars. The sizes of both the?Scale ?and?Bias ?properties?are singleton except for the last dimension (for example, [1 1 1 5]). The last dimension of the Scale ?and?Bias ?properties correspond to any dimension except the channel dimension.  For example, generating code for the following network might produce incorrect results: dlIn = dlarray(ones(2,3,4,5,6,'single'), 'SSCBT');scale = reshape(1:5, [1 1 1 5]); % the last dimension of scale corresponds to the 'B' dimensionbias = scale;dlnet = dlnetwork([scalingLayer(Scale=scale,Bias=bias)], dlIn);
Workaround: Manually expand the Scale or Bias?property along at least one other dimension.For example, you can expand Scale along the height dimension, while specifying the same numeric scale and bias values to the layer:dlIn = dlarray(ones(2,3,4,5,6,'single'), 'SSCBT');scale = reshape([1:5;1:5], [2 1 1 5]);bias = scale;dlnet = dlnetwork([scalingLayer(Scale=scale,Bias=bias)], dlIn);

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reusable subsystems with Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might generate inadequate code to protect interpolation
Description: Reusable subsystems with Interpolation using Prelookup blocks might generate inadequate code to protect interpolation when all of the following conditions are true:? A model has multiple identical atomic subsystems set to reuse code if possible. The subsystems have Interpolation using Prelookup blocks whose extrapolation methods are set to Clip .  In the first instance of the subsystem, the Interpolation using Prelookup block takes input from external Prelookup block(s) whose extrapolation methods are set to Clip.  In at least one other instance of the subsystem, the Interpolation using Prelookup block takes input from a block other than a?Prelookup block. These non-Prelookup blocks feed out-of-range index or fraction values to the Interpolation using Prelookup block.  Execution of the generated code might produce incorrect results for the latter because the code will not have protection against the out-of-range index or fraction values.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Incomplete results after normal or accelerator mode simulation run using?Simulation?object if SimulationOutput?property was queried during simulation
Description: The SimulationOutput ?property of the  Simulation ?object returns incomplete results after normal or accelerator mode simulation completes if you queried the  SimulationOutput ?property during the simulation. ?  If you do not query the SimulationOutput ?property value during simulation, the property returns complete simulation results after a normal or accelerator mode simulation completes.?  This bug does not apply to rapid accelerator simulations or simulations deployed using Simulink Compiler.?

Summary: Signal property discrepancy unreported when implicit resolution is active
Description: When you use implicit resolution to a Simulink.Signal ? object, a discrepancy between the property of a resolved signal object (for example Datatype, Complexity or Dimensions) and the property value of the corresponding signal, as propagated during model compilation, might not be reported under these conditions:The signal object is in the model or base workspace The model has attached data dictionaries
Workaround: Enable explicit signal resolution by enabling the model configuration parameter Signal name must resolve to Simulink signal object.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Inconsistent initial conditions for multi-instance subsystem output ports might produce incorrect results
Description: The code generator might generate incorrect code for a model that meets these conditions:The model contains a multi-instance subsystem that has inconsistent initial conditions for the output port. For example, one instance has initial condition 1  while other instance has initial condition 2  for the output port.  The output port is connected to a Delay or Memory block inside the subsystem.

Summary: Model with For Iterator subsystem inside Terminate/Reinitialize Function block produces incorrect accelerator mode simulation results
Description: If you have a model with a For Iterator subsystem block inside a Terminate Function block or a Reinitialize?Function block, simulating the model in accelerator mode produces incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Simulate the model in normal or rapid accelerator mode.

Summary: Parameterized base workspace variables in conditional expressions do not use parameterized values
Description: When you define a design study in the Multiple Simulations panel that parameterizes a base workspace variable in?a conditional expression, the conditional evaluates the variable using only the initial value of base workspace variable and ignores the parameterized values that you specify.

Summary: Assertion error during HDL code generation when you change Target workflow from IP Core Generation to Generic ASIC/FPGA??
Description: When you generate HDL code using the HDL Workflow Advisor for a model containing a MATLAB function block with HDL Architecture  set to MATLAB Datapath ?you get an assertion error when your workflow includes the following steps in this order:?Open the HDL Workflow Advisor? Select IP Core Generation  as the Target workflow  in the?Set Target Device and Synthesis Tool ?task? Set your Target platform  and Run the?Set Target Device and Synthesis Tool ?task Change the Target workflow  to Generic ASIC/FPGA  in the?Set Target Device and Synthesis Tool ?task Right-click the?Generate RTL Code and Testbench ?task and select?Run to Selected Task. You will see the following assertion:?  Failed? Error:Assertion failed.  Error using?hdlcoder.pirctx/earlyElaborate ?  Error:Assertion failed.?
Workaround: After getting the assertion:Set the?Target workflow?to?Generic ASIC/FPGA?in the?Set Target Device and Synthesis Tool?task?in the HDL Workflow Advisor.?Right click the Set Target Device and Synthesis Tool task and select Reset This TaskClose the HDL Workflow AdvisorSave the modelClose the modelDelete the Project folder ( if you did not change it, the default it is 'hdl_prj')Reopen the modelOpen the HDL Workflow Advisor, select?No?if prompted to Auto-restore the last exited session?Right-click the?Generate RTL Code and Testbench?task then select?Run to Selected Task.

Summary: jsonencode incorrectly encodes elements that are equal to -realmax('single')
Description: When using jsonencode  to create JSON-formatted text from a large single value, MATLAB will produce the incorrect answer when called with a value equal to the output of -realmax('single') . ?>> jsonencode(-realmax("single"))  ans =  ? ? '-3.402823466E+3'   The correct answer is??'-3. 402823466E+38' .

Summary: Protected model with tunable parameters might error when compiling
Description: Compiling a model hierarchy that contains a protected model might produce an error when all these conditions apply:? The protected model is being used in a different release or update release than the release used to create it. The protected model has one or more tunable parameters. The protected model is using the same variables that existed when the protected model was created.  The software might not recognize that the variables are the same. The error message is similar to this: One or more global variables used by protected model "myModel.slxp" are incompatible with the variables that existed when the protected model was created. Incompatible global variables: #myVariable.sldd#

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect binary generation for FMU Version 2.0 with automatic data type conversion for single data types
Description: A model exported to an FMU compatible with FMI version 2.0 produces incorrect simulation output when automatic data type conversion is enabled for single  data types at ports.

Summary: Simulink.LookupTable objects unable to share struct?data?type
Description: Multiple? Simulink.LookupTable ?objects are unable to share?struct ?data? types in generated code when the?PreserveDimensions ?storage class property is selected.? For example, for these two??Simulink.LookupTable ?objects,  lut1  and  lut2 ,? the objects share the?struct ?data type, but MATLAB throws an error when you try to generate code. lut1.Table.Value=[1 2 3]  lut2.Table.Value=[2 4 6]?
Workaround: Configure each Simulink.LookupTable?object with a unique Name?value for the property StructTypeInfo.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might not initialize global variables that represent outputs of reusable subsystems
Description: The generated code might not initialize global outputs for a subsystem when all ?of the following are true: The Enable local block outputs ?model configuration parameter is selected. The model contains multiple subsystems that can be optimized into a single reusable function in the generated code. Each of those subsystems has outputs that have initial values. Some, but not all, of those subsystem outputs are unconnected or attached to a Terminator block.  The code generator generates a reusable function for the subsystems. For the outputs that are unconnected or attached to a Terminator block, the generated code uses local variables. For the outputs that are connected to another block, the generated code uses global variables. The code generator might not set the initial values for global variables that represent connected outputs. This is incorrect behavior because the generated code should initialize global variables that have initial values.
Workaround: Set the PassReuseOutputArgsAs model configuration parameter to Structure reference. To do this programmatically, run this command from the MATLAB Command Window, substituting modelName?with the name of your model:set_param(modelName, 'PassReuseOutputArgsAs', 'Structure reference')To set the parameter in the UI, set the Configuration Parameters > Code Generation > Optimization > Pass reusable subsystem outputs as?model configuration parameter?to Structure reference.

Summary: A2L file generation might cause MATLAB to crash during runtime for a model with a lookup table block that has values for both table data and breakpoints from the same member of a Simulink bus object
Description: The?coder.asap2.export ?API parses data from a Simulink model and accumulates it into a structured format as required for the A2L file generation. When a model with a lookup table block has values for both table data and breakpoints from the same member of a Simulink bus object, data accessed by?coder.asap2.export()  is incorrect which? might cause MATLAB to crash during runtime. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [0] 0x00007ff87cbbb1c2 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\slcg_mf.dll+06140354 coder::descriptor::types::Matrix::Impl::getBaseType+00000018[1] 0x00007ff9132b7b35 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\asap2_util.dll+00752437 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00601365[2] 0x00007ff9132b9d3d C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\asap2_util.dll+00761149 mwboost::serialization::singleton_module::unlock+00610077
Workaround: For values in lookup table blocks, use either explicit data or different members of a Simulink bus object for table data and breakpoints.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reinitialized triggered subsystem might generate incorrect code
Description: A triggered subsystem might generate incorrect code when all these conditions are met: The triggered subsystem is reinitialized. the triggered subsystem has Function Packaging ?set to Auto ?or  Inline.  The Trigger block in the triggered subsystem has?Trigger type ?set to?rising ?or? either . The Trigger block in the triggered subsystem has?Initial trigger signal state ?set to?zero ?or? negative .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: fmincon for code generation has poor performance, appears stalled, or returns incorrect exitflag and lambda?values
Description: fmincon  for code generation has two issues:  might appear to stall, or exhibit poor performance. might return an incorrect lambda  value and report the solution as feasible (exitflag  = 2) but not optimal (exitflag  = 1), even when the returned solution is optimal.    There is no known way to predict whether a problem will be affected by these issues.

Summary: Constant folding error for the Fcn block when using Simulink.Parameter object in the block expression.
Description: The Fcn block errors during HDL code generation if Simulink.Parameter object is used in the block expression. HDL code generation throws the following error message:? Internal Error: Evaluation of validate function on class hdldefaults.Fcn failed with the error message: : Constant folding error: ConstAnnotator.run: 30: Input matrix must be a numeric array, character array, or string array.
Workaround: Use a workspace variable instead of a Simulink.Parameter object: either assign the value directly to the variable (x = 3) or reassign the parameter's value field to the variable (x = myParam.Value). This assumes the parameter value is a numeric constant and not an expression.?

Summary: The function?mvncdf?might incorrectly error when passed gpuArray inputs
Description: The function? mvncdf ?might incorrectly throw the following error when passed  gpuArray  inputs containing some edge case values: ?Error using log  LOG: needs to return a complex result,  but this is not supported for real input X on the GPU.  Use LOG(COMPLEX(X)) instead.

Summary: Rapid Accelerator simulation that uses runtime diagnostics might produce uncompilable C++ code
Description: Model simulation might not start and generated C++ code might fail to compile when all ?of the following are true: The model uses the Rapid Accelerator simulation mode The model contains a block that uses runtime diagnostics such as the Check for out-of-range index in accelerated simulation ?parameter of a Selector block  The compiler fails with an error similar to the following:  error: invalid conversion from 'const void*' to 'void*'

Summary: Multithreaded LU decomposition of a dense matrix on Apple Silicon can return wrong answers
Description: If you compute the LU decomposition of a dense matrix on an Apple Silicon machine that is running natively-built MATLAB, the answers you receive can sporadically be wrong. This affects multithreaded calculations using the?lu ?and?inv ?functions, the?decomposition ?object, and the?\ ?operator. This issue does not affect Intel-based MATLAB that is run via Rosetta2.?
Workaround: Restrict multithreading of MATLAB to circumvent the problem. You can achieve this with one of the following:Call the following command after starting MATLAB:>> maxNumCompThreads(1)Start MATLAB with the option "-singleCompThread", as in:$ matlab -singleCompThreadNote that both workarounds drastically decrease performance of MATLAB.

Summary: HDL code generation throws an internal error when the control port of the Multiport Switch block has an array input with a word length greater than 64 bits.
Description: When the input to the control port of the Multiport Switch block is an array which has word length greater than 64 bits, HDL code generation throws the following internal error: Code generaiton failed with the error message : Unrecognized method, property, or field 'Signed' for class 'hdlcoder.tp_array' ??

Summary: HDL Coder / HDL Operations blocks?Serializer1D and Deserializer1D show incorrect block descriptions in HDL Block Properties dialog box
Description: Serializer1D block has an incorrect block description "Convert from scalar to vector, or from smaller-size vector to larger-size vector. The output rate is S / (Ratio + Idle Cycles), where S is the input rate." ?in the HDL Block Properties dialog box. Serializer1D block converts vector signal to scalar or smaller vectors. So block description shown is incorrect.? Similarly, Deserializer1D block has an incorrect block description "Convert from vector to scalar, or to smaller-size vector. The output rate is V * (Ratio + Idle Cycles), where V is the input rate." ?in the HDL Block Properties dialog box.?Deserializer1D block? converts scalar stream or smaller vectors to vector signals.

Summary: C Function block might produce parsing error during code generation with AUTOSAR or custom data type targets
Description: Generating code from C Function block with AUTOSAR or custom datatype targets might produce an error similar to the following. ?Error using tlc_c   ?Parsing Output Code in C Function block <blockPath> failed with the messages:   ?In line :error: 20, identifier "uint32" is undefined

Summary: Build log from SIL/PIL simulation incorrectly reports Compilation artifacts were out of date
Description: Suppose code is already generated and up to date, and you run a top-model software-in-the-loop (SIL) or processor-in-the-loop (PIL) simulation for a second time, the build log indicates that top-model artifacts were out of date. For example:? BuildSummary  Topmodel targets built:  Model?????Action????????? RebuildReason? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ??  ====================================================================  mBuildTop?Code compiled.? Compilation artifacts were out of date.?    Although the compilation artifacts for the top model were not modified by the second build process, the log incorrectly displays this Rebuild Reason  message: Compilation artifacts were out of date.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Same referenced model instance inside and outside conditional subsystem might produce incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect for a model that has an instance of a referenced model inside a conditional subsystem and an instance outside of it when all the following conditions are met: The conditional subsystem receives a variable-size signal at its input port. The conditional subsystem is disabled. The  Propagate?sizes of variable-size signals ?parameter for the control block of the conditional subsystem?is set to?Only when enabling .  The Model block inside the subsystem is set to simulate in normal mode and outputs variable-size signals. The Model block directly connects to the Outport block of the conditional subsystem. The top-level model is set to run in normal mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder may generate incorrect code when a reused subsystem in a feedback loop introduces more latency than the available latency budget in the loop
Description: The generated HDL code may be incorrect when all  these conditions are met:The model contains identical Simulink subsystems that generate a single file due to code reuse. At least one of these reused subsystems is part of a feedback loop. The reused subsystem(s) within the loop introduces latency that exceeds the available latency budget (design delays) in the loop.
Workaround: To verify the correctness of the generated HDL code:Generate a validation model.Check if there is any numerical mismatch.If mismatches are found, increase the design delays in the feedback loop to absorb the extra latency. If mismatches persist, refer to the Delay Balancing report to identify any model or block settings that may be preventing the extra latency from being absorbed. Note that this information is available from R2023b onwards.

Summary: Traceability Diagram does not show cleared change issues correctly
Description: After you clear a change issue for a link, the Traceability Diagram  continues to show that the link has a change issue.

Summary: Trace matrix for Simulink model comparison incorrectly references script hRptEvalXMLQualkit.m
Description: Artifact?Simulink Model Comparison Trace Matrix  (mathworks_slcmp_trace .xlsx ) incorrectly references file hRptEvalXMLQualkit.m , which does not ship with the DO Qualification Kit.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check fails with error: When keys are specified, values must also be specified
Description: SimulinkDesign Verifier compatibility check fails and throws the following error: When keys are specified,values must also be specified?  Thishappens when the model consists of following types of blocks:  Model Reference   Data Store Memory  Data Store Read? and/or?DataStore Write    For such a model, the blockreplacement API?sldvblockreplacement ?also fails with thesame error.

Summary: Incorrect label Interpreter?value when loading .mlapp and .mat files
Description: When loading .mlapp and .mat files saved in R2023a or earlier releases, the Interpreter  property of labels created using the uilabel ?function is always reset to the default value, 'none' .

Summary: Incorrect HDL code is generated for Gain block that has the vector input, but the input vector orientation is not aligned with the gain factor.
Description: HDL Coder generates incorrect HDL code for the Gain block when you configure the Gain block with following settings:? Input to the block is vector.? Multiplication  mode is?Element-wise(K.*u).?  Orientation of input vector ( column/row) and the Gain  factor (row/column) are not aligned.  The generated HDL Code might fail to compile in synthesis tools.
Workaround: Ensure that the orientation of the gain input and the gain factor is consistent. Either configure both as the row vectors or column vectors.?

Summary: Vector signals with faults can produce erroneous outputs
Description: If you simulate faults on a vector signal and the fault behavior breaks the signal between the Fault Inport and Fault Outport blocks, such as with Stuck-At-Ground and Stuck-At-Constant behavior, the fault might inject in unexpected ways, resulting in erroneous simulation data.
