Summary: Selected Align > Toggle Snap to option not shown in Signal Editor context menu
Description: The Align  option in the Signal Editor context menu does not indicate the currently active Toggle Snap to...  ?option.

Summary: Signal Editor workflows break when converting signal data type to or from enumeration
Description: In the Signal Editor, converting the signal data type to or from enumeration breaks editing workflows. For example, signal points are no longer plotted correctly.
Workaround: If the plots are not correct, double click the signal's visualize column (eye icon in the signal tree) to unplot. Then double click again to replot the signal whose data type was changed.

Summary: Searching for text in the Comparison Tool might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Searching for text in the Comparison Tool might cause MATLAB to crash if?all ?the following conditions are true: You compare two Simulink Test MLDATX files, Requirements or Link Sets files, Polyspace Project files, or System Composer architecture files. You click Merge Mode . You click Refresh  or Swap Sides .

Summary: Compatibility check might fail for a Simulink Model with Initialize?subsystem which writes to Data Store Memory.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check might fail for Simulink models in which both?Initialize? subsystem and?Function-Call Subsystem ?write to same?Data store memory ?block. It throws the following error message:?Failed to honor block sorted order for?Initialize? task during block replacement.  The same error message can appear when you use?sldvblockreplacement  on the model? to replace the blocks for analysis.

Summary: Frequently setting UI component properties might crash or stall long-running apps
Description: Any app that frequently updates components will consume increasing memory over time, possibly leading to a crash. You can observe this issue in the memory data of Task Manager on Windows, or Activity Monitor on Mac, or any process monitoring app on your Linux distribution.

Summary: Schedule Editor might incorrectly display partitions for AUTOSAR architecture as scheduable
Description: The Schedule Editor might incorrectly display partitions of an AUTOSAR software architecture model as schedulable if the following conditions are true: The architecture model contains Basic Software (BSW) service blocks. The architecture model does not contain components or compositions linked to implementation models.  Additionally, if a model block in a Simulink modelreferences the AUTOSAR architecture model, the Simulink model might have input ports that you cannot schedule.?
Workaround: Link an implementation model to a component in the AUTOSAR software architecture model.

Summary: The Design and Simulate Tracking Scenario with Tracking Scenario Designer Example session MAT-file leads to an error when adding more platforms.
Description: After running the following code in the? Design and Simulate Tracking Scenario with Tracking Scenario Designer Example  to launch the Tracking Scenario Designer app:  >>trackingScenarioDesigner('TSD_TrackingCloselySpacedTargets.mat') Attempting to add another platform results in an error dialog containing the following text: 'Unrecognized field name "RCSSignature".' ?
Workaround: Launch the app without any input:>> trackingScenarioDesignerAfter the app has opened, load this session from the toolstrip by clicking on Open and selecting the TSD_TrackingCloselySpacedTargets MAT-file containing the saved tracking scenario session.

Summary: Internal error during simulation on?Apple silicon devices
Description: In some rare cases, Simulink might halt simulation and report the following error: Internal Error: Please see MathWorks Bug Report 3166498.   The error might occur when Simulink is creating internal artifacts to use for simulation and / or code generation. The probability of seeing the error increases when the computer is under heavy memory usage.
Workaround: The following actions might resolve the error:?Close all unnecessary applicationsRestart your MATLABReboot your computer

Summary: UDP Send block ignores UDP packet size parameter value
Description: The UDP Send block parameter "'UDP packet size'' setting is ignored, and not changing the packet size as expected. The size of the sent packet is always the default 512 bytes.? This issue also occurs when programmatically setting the OutputDatagramPacketSize  parameter.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Floating-point results mismatch when using Xcode 14.3 or later?toolchain on Mac with Apple Silicon
Description: Xcode 14.3 and later versions of this toolchain enable FMA (Fused-Multiply Add) by default on Mac with Apple Silicon (Xcode 14.3 Release Notes | Apple Developer Documentation ), which causes MATLAB function execution results to be numerically different from the results generated as part of these workflows: Execution of MEX file generated from?C/C++ ?file Code generation using MATLAB Coder or Embedded Coder SIL/PIL simulation using Embedded Coder   In most cases, the differences are within floating-point tolerance. However, depending on the algorithm, small changes might become magnified into significant differences.?  For example, assume you have a .c  file named mexIssue.c , as shown below: #include <math.h>  #include "mex.h"  double mexIssue()  {  ? double in1 = 0.84;  ? double in2 = 0.54;  ? double in3 = -0.84;  ? double in4 = 0.54;  ? return floor(in3 * in2 + in4 * in1);  }  void mexFunction(int nlhs, mxArray* plhs[], int nrhs, const mxArray* prhs[]) {  ? ? double* y;  ? ? plhs[0] = mxCreateDoubleMatrix((mwSize)1, (mwSize)1, mxREAL);  ? ? y = mxGetPr(plhs[0]);  ? ? *y = mexIssue();  }?  If you?generate a MEX file from the .c  file above via this command:? mex mexIssue.c  and then execute the generated MEX file by calling mexIssue ?via the? MATLAB command line, you will get a result of -1. However, the expected result is zero.
Workaround: For the MEX workflow where you generate a MEX file from a C/C++ file, you can manually add compiler flags to turn off FMA:For C:?? ? ? ? ? ? mex -v CFLAGS='$CFLAGS -ffp-contract=off' <fileName.c>?For C++:?? ? ? ? ? ??mex -v CXXFLAGS='$CXXFLAGS -ffp-contract=off' <fileName.cpp>

Summary: Model block with initialize, reinitialize, reset, or terminate port produces incorrect simulation results in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Simulation results might be incorrect if all the following conditions occur:? The model is set to simulate in rapid accelerator mode. The model contains a Model block with?Show model initialize port  ,?Show model reinitialize ports,? Show model reset ports  , or Show model terminate port   option selected. The Model block has Schedule rates   option deselected. The top model uses variable step solver or has the configuration parameter Treat each discrete rate as a separate task  deselected. Alternatively,?get_param(model,'SolverMode') ?returns?'SingleTasking' . The referenced model contains only one discrete periodic sample time. The signal driving the initialize, reinitialize, reset, or terminate port of the Model block has different discrete periodic sample time than the discrete periodic sample time set for the referenced model.  You might observe that the Model block executes on the sample hits that are common in both the signal driving the initialize, reset, or terminate port, and the referenced model, instead of executing solely on the sample hits set for the referenced model.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Disabling floating-point configuration using command-line interface may?trigger a segv error when using?makehdl()
Description: An internal segv  error may occur with makehdl()  if the floating-point configuration is disabled using this command: hdlset_param(gcs, 'UseFloatingPoint', 'off')  This problem is specific to deactivating the floating-point configuration via the 'UseFloatingPoint ' command-line interface. Disabling it through the Configuration Parameters dialog box does not cause this issue.
Workaround: Disabling Floating Point Configuration through the 'Configuration Parameters' user interface can avoid this issue.

Summary: Compatibility check might fail when an inactive variant choice in a model?contains Model blocks.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check might fail? and throws following error when an inactive variant choice in a model consists of Model  blocks: The specified key is not present in this container.  For such a model, the block replacement parameter?sldvblockreplacement ?might also fail with the same error.?

Summary: Test case IDs in the Simulink Test trace matrix do not match the IDs provided in the title for the test case in Test Manager
Description: When viewed by using the Simulink Test Test Manager, the ID identified in the title of the test case does not match the test case ID in the Simulink Test trace matrix. Affected tests include: tCov1  tCov2  tCov3  tCov4  tCov5  tCov6  tCov7  tCov8  tCov9  tCov11  tCov12  tCov13  tCov14?
Workaround: Update the test case IDs in artifact Simulink Test - Trace Matrix to match the IDs in the title of the test case.?Depending on the release version, use trace matrix file:? ?For R2023a and earlier, use qualkitdo_sltest_trace.xlsxFor R2023b and later, use mathworks_sltest_trace.xlsxIn the trace matrix, open these worksheets and update the ID:?tCov1, replace SLT_COV_010 with SLT_COV_001tCov2, replace SLT_COV_020 with SLT_COV_002tCov3, replace SLT_COV_030 with SLT_COV_003tCov4, replace SLT_COV_040 with SLT_COV_004Cov5, replace SLT_COV_050 with SLT_COV_005tCov6, replace SLT_COV_060 with SLT_COV_006tCov7, replace SLT_COV_070 with SLT_COV_007tCov8, replace SLT_COV_080 with SLT_COV_008tCov9, replace SLT_COV_090 with SLT_COV_009tCov11, replace SLT_COV_020 with SLT_COV_011tCov12, replace SLT_COV_050 with SLT_COV_012tCov13, replace SLT_COV_080 with SLT_COV_013tCov14, replace SLT_COV_090 with SLT_COV_014
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Vehicles at rest might start to move unexpectedly in models that use the Tire (Magic Formula) block or the Rolling Resistance block
Description: For the Tire (Magic Formula) block and the Rolling Resistance block, when you set?Rolling Resistance ?to? Pressure and velocity dependent (Magic Formula) , the block might unexpectedly generate a force that propels a stationary vehicle.
Workaround: Set?Rolling Resistance?to?Constant coefficient or Pressure and velocity dependent (SAE J2452).

Summary: MATLAB crashes when saving an architecture model that uses subsystem reference components
Description: MATLAB might crash when saving an architecture model when: Using an architecture subsystem reference (a component linked to an architecture subsystem file). Using another subsystem reference inside a subsystem reference (nested subsystem references).

Summary: License Manager Error 4402 "Missing root element in response"
Description: You might not be able to login to MATLAB using Online Licensing in R2023b with: MathWorks containers on DockerHub MATLAB Integration for Jupyter matlab-proxy?based integrations  Attempts to login might be presented with the following error messages: Starting MATLAB with license: 40759245 - MATLAB (Individual)License checkout failed.License Manager Error 4402Missing root element in response Troubleshoot this issue by visiting: https://www.mathworks.com/support/lme/4402Diagnostic Information:Feature: MATLAB License path:??Licensing error: 4402, 0.

Summary: Running createInputDataset on model containing?Simulink.Parameter?with Unit property set causes warnings on command line
Description: When you run?createInputDataset ?on a model containing Simulink.Parameter  with its?Unit ?property set causes warnings in the command line. The warning message refers to the units database and the temporary model that is created during simulation, for example: Warning: Units 'eKnots' specified for the 'Unit' field of Simulink.Parameter object 'Wind_DowndraftWindSpeed' referenced by'TmpModelForCreatingInportGroundValues' are not defined in the Simulink unit database. Investigate 12 similar inconsistencies on Model Advisor?Report.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when saving a subsystem file with a Goto block referenced in an architecture model
Description: MATLAB might crash when saving a subsystem file when both of the following conditions are true:?There is Goto block inside a Simulink subsystem reference. The Goto block is set to 'Global' visibility setting.

Summary: Importing and cancelling spreadsheet and MAT-file into Root Inport Mapper causes busy icon
Description: Linking and cancelling spreadsheets with the Root Inport Mapper tool might cause a busy icon. The issue occurs with this sequence: In Root Inport Mapper toolbar, click From Spreadsheet .? In the From Spreadsheet dialog, click Browse ?and then click Cancel . Close or cancel the From Spreadsheet dialog. In Root Inport Mapper toolbar, click?From MAT-File . In the From MAT-File dialog, click?Browse ?and then click Cancel .  The busy icon also occurs if you start the sequence with?From MAT-File ?and then From Spreadsheet .

Summary: Unable to set the Simulink cache and code generation folders to the same location when using the Project API
Description: Setting the Simulink cache and code generation folders?does not correctly?update the folders' location if both  of these?conditions are true: You use the Project API to set the Simulink cache and code generation folders. You use the same location for both folders.  For example, the following commands only set the?Simulink cache folder ?correctly. The? Simulink code generation?folder does not update correctly. proj = currentProject();proj.SimulinkCacheFolder = "folder";proj.SimulinkCodeGenFolder = "folder";
Workaround: Update the Simulink cache and code generation folders using the Project user interface instead.Open the project.In the?Project?tab, click?Details.In the?Generated Simulink Files?section, set the Simulink cache and code generation folders to the desired location.

Summary: Audio plugin generation fails to build plugins generated using Xcode 15
Description: When generating a plugin using Xcode 15, a build error occurs. For example, the code: generateAudioPlugin audiopluginexample.Echo produces a long error message containing the following: error: Cycle inside Echo - VST; building could produce unreliable results.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Call to dlarray method softmax?that includes?'DataFormat'?name-value argument
Description: In the generated code, a call to the?dlarray  method?softmax ?might produce incorrect results if the call uses the 'DataFormat' ?name-value argument and either  of these conditions is true:  The value of the input has singleton  U ?dimensions. For example:    dlIn = dlarray(ones(5,4,1,3));out = softmax(dlIn,'DataFormat','CTUB');  The input has an internal permutation. For example:  dlIn = dlarray(ones(5,4,3));out = softmax(permute(dlIn,[3 1 2]),'DataFormat','TCB');
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Reciprocal block with?ShifAdd architecture might have simulation mismatch when output data type is having negative fractional length
Description: The generated HDL code for the Reciprocal block that has HDL?Architecture  set to ShiftAdd ?  might have simulation mismatch if both the conditions are true:?  Output data type  is fixed-point that has negative fractional length.? Sum of output data type fraction length and input datatype fraction length is negative.?

Summary: Test Generation analysis incorrectly reports objectives as Unsatisfiable on a model with dirty subsystem references.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier reports Invalid Simulink object handle  error  if you create a harness for a subsystem reference and perform test generation analysis without saving the subsystem reference Subsequently, if you save the subsystem reference and rerun the analysis, Simulink Design Verifier incorrectly reports all objectives from that subsystem reference as Unsatisfiable . This erroneous behavior is noticed only when the parameter?Rebuild model representation ?is set to If change is detected.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Data brushing and data tips are unresponsive in certain tabs of apps
Description: If you create an app containing a tab group that is not visible when the app launches, app users will not be able to apply data brushing or create data tips in any charts that exist in that tab group.

Summary: crossentropy, huber, l1loss, and l2loss return incorrect values for masked loss
Description: When you set the NormalizationFactor  option of crossentropy , huber , l1loss , and l2loss  to "mask-included" , the functions do not divide by the number of observations when normalizing the loss values.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Clock-rate pipelining with hierarchical feedback loops may result in assertion during HDL code generation
Description: HDL Coder may throw an internal assertion when generating HDL code when a model has all   these:? Feedback loops through hierarchy. Feedback loops modeled at a rate slower than the clock-rate. Clock-rate pipelining is enabled.  Unexpected error during HDL Code Generation. Contact support@mathworks.com with reproduction steps. Assertion failed with debug message: matlab\src\cgir_hdl\pir_transforms\MatchingDelayReducer.cpp:291:c != m_nw->getOutportComp()
Workaround: Enabling hierarchy flattening on the design or at least the feedback loop regions may avoid the assertion.hdlset_param(<path_to_DUT>, 'FlattenHierarchy', 'on')?

Summary: Comparison Tool throws an error when selecting MLAPP files to compare
Description: Selecting MLAPP files using the Select Files or Folder for Comparison  dialog issues the following error in the Command Window: Unable to resolve the name 'com.mathworks.toolbox.matlab.appdesigner.comparison.MlappComparisonType'.Error in comparisons.internal.dispatcherutil.getJComparisonType (line 49)            jType = com.mathworks.toolbox.matlab.appdesigner.comparison.MlappComparisonType();Error in comparisons.internal.getJComparisonTypesViaMATLAB>addJComparisonType (line 28)    jType = comparisons.internal.dispatcherutil.getJComparisonType(provider.getType());Error in comparisons.internal.getJComparisonTypesViaMATLAB>@(provider)addJComparisonType(provider,jTypes) (line 20)        arrayfun(@(provider) addJComparisonType(provider, jTypes), availableProviders(inds));Error in comparisons.internal.getJComparisonTypesViaMATLAB (line 20)        arrayfun(@(provider) addJComparisonType(provider, jTypes), availableProviders(inds));
Workaround: Compare MLAPP files using the context menu option or the?visdiff?function instead.In the Current Folder Browser, select two MLAPP files, then right-click and select Compare Selected Files/Folders.In the Command Window, enter:visdiff(firstMlappFilePath,secondMlappFilePath);

Summary: extractFileText fails to extract text from PDF
Description: extractFileText  might skip parts or all of the text in some PDF files where the internal structure contains one or more of the following: XObjects using the same name as other XObjects? XObjects that do not have a /Type  entry
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Setting InputPipeline on subsystem introduces twice the number of latency cycles than expected
Description: When HDL block property InputPipeline  is set to X, HDL Coder introduces X cycles of latency. However, it may introduce 2X cycles of latency under all  these conditions: InputPipeline  is configured for the subsystem that is supposed to be flattened. This means that FlattenHierarchy  is set to on  for either the subsystem itself or one of its parent subsystems. A fan-out signal, which drives multiple inputs of the subsystem, is present.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: rigidBodyTree functions produce silent wrong answers or unexpected errors after using?replaceJoint or replaceBody.
Description: Using the?replaceJoint ?or?replaceBody ?functions to replace bodies or joints of a rigid body tree?leads to incorrect results or unexpected errors when using rigid body tree kinematic or dynamic functions. These failure cases can occur on a subset of rigid body trees for which any of the following is true:?? The user has called?replaceJoint ?to replace a fixed joint on rigid body tree with a non-fixed joint. The user has called?replaceBody ?to replace a body, resulting in a decrease in the number of fixed joints in the rigid body tree.?
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:?Workaround 1: Replace rigid bodies by removing the desired rigid body and then adding a subtree using the?removeBody?and?addSubtree functions.?Code snippet below illustrates how to replace the first body's joint to prismatic on a rigid body tree with two revolute joints.?% A rigid body tree with two revolute jointsrbt=twoJointRigidBodyTree;% Create a copy of the body in the tree which should be replaced.bodyNameToBeReplaced='body1';bodyInRBTToBeReplaced=getBody(rbt,bodyNameToBeReplaced);bodyToBeReplaced=copy(bodyInRBTToBeReplaced);% Note the name of the parent body.parentNameOfBodyToBeReplaced=bodyInRBTToBeReplaced.Parent.Name;% Remove the subtrees associated with the children bodies of the body to be% replaced, and remove the body to be replaced.childrenOfBodyInRBTToBeReplaced=bodyInRBTToBeReplaced.Children;childrenSubtrees={};for c=1:length(childrenOfBodyInRBTToBeReplaced)? ? childrenSubtrees{end+1}=removeBody(rbt,childrenOfBodyInRBTToBeReplaced{c}.Name);endremoveBody(rbt,bodyNameToBeReplaced);% Modify the joint of the body1.bodyToBeReplaced.Joint=rigidBodyJoint("joint1", "prismatic");% Reconstruct the treeaddBody(rbt,bodyToBeReplaced,parentNameOfBodyToBeReplaced);for subt=childrenSubtrees? ? addSubtree(rbt,bodyToBeReplaced.Name,subt{1})end% View details of the original twoJointRigidBodyTree and the newly modifed tree% to verify the result of operations.showdetails(twoJointRigidBodyTree);showdetails(rbt);Workaround 2:??Use?writeAsFunction?to output an M file containing the function to generate the rigid body tree, and then edit the function to make appropriate changes to joints or bodies in the tree.

Summary: HDL Coder does not realize DUT ports for tunable parameters declared in the model workspace.??
Description: When you use tunable parameters that are declared in the model workspace for realizing the DUT ports in your model, HDL Coder ignores these tunable parameters and generates the HDL Code by treating them as constants. HDL code generation is successful without any warning or error.
Workaround: Instead of declaring tunable parameters in the model workspace, create tunable parameters inside MATLAB Base workspace or inside a data dictionary attached with the model.?

Summary: ?programming language reserved identifier?error when same int64 or uint64 alias used for?Data Type Replacement?and for block or signal output
Description: Suppose you use Simulink.AliasType  to create an alias for int64   named MyInt64Type ? and these conditions apply: The alias is used as a replacement name in the Code Generation > Data Type Replacement  pane. For example, running the command get_param(modelName, 'ReplacementTypes').int64  returns 'MyInt64Type' .  The alias is used to specify the data type of a block output or signal in the model. For example, running the command get_param(blockPath, 'OutDataTypeStr')  returns 'MyInt64Type' . ?  ?When you generate code for the model, you might see an error like this: Unable to generate code using user-defined data type 'Myint64' because it conflicts with a programming language reserved identifier?
Workaround: Modify the block diagram as follows: If you use a Simulink.AliasType MyInt64Type for int64, replace instances of MyInt64Type for block output or signal data types with?int64.If you use a?Simulink.AliasType?MyUint64Type?for uint64, replace instances of?MyUint64Type?for block output or signal data types with?uint64.The generated code will continue to use MyInt64Type and MyUint64Type provided the?Code Generation > Data Type Replacement?pane still specifies the aliases.

Summary: Counterbalance Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization might cause unexpected behavior
Description: The Counterbalance Valve (IL) block pre-parameterization options from the manufacturer Sun Hydraulics do not extrapolate the supplier data sheet to the full relief stage opening. This can result in incorrect results during a load lowering simulation scenario in heavy machinery.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Floating-point results mismatch when using Xcode 14.3 or later?toolchain on Mac with Apple Silicon
Description: Xcode 14.3 and later versions of this toolchain enable FMA (Fused-Multiply Add) by default on Mac with Apple Silicon (Xcode14.3 Release Notes | Apple Developer Documentation ), which causes normal mode simulation results to be numerically different from the results generated as part of these workflows:Accelerator, rapid-accelerator, and external-mode simulation? C/C++ ?code generation using Simulink Coder or Embedded Coder? SIL/PIL simulation using Embedded Coder   In most cases, the differences are within floating-point tolerance. However, depending on the algorithm, small changes might be magnified into significant differences. For example, when the output of a Sine Wave block feeds into a Ceil block, there will be non-trivial differences between the generated code and normal mode simulation results.

Summary: AUTOSAR software component with provide-require port (PRPort) can produce mismatch between model and SIL/PIL simulation results
Description: Suppose you use the SIL/PIL Manager to test code generated from a model that implements?AUTOSAR port-based sender-receiver (S-R) communication. You might observe a numerical mismatch between model and SIL/PIL simulation results when these conditions apply: An AUTOSAR software component uses a sender-receiver interface. The component has root-level Inport and Outport blocks that are mapped to provide-require ports (PRPort ) with 'DataAccessMode ' set to 'ImplicitReceive ' and?'ImplicitSend ' respectively. Within a single execution of the runnable, the generated code reads from an input PRPort  after writing to the corresponding output PRPort . For example:?  void Runnable(void)  {     ? ...   ? Rte_IWrite_Runnable_PRPort1_PRPort1(x);   ? ...  ? y = Rte_IRead_Runnable_PRPort1_PRPort1();  }?

Summary: FloatingPointTargetConfig object accepts invalid property value and cause downstream failure in HDL Workflow Advisor.
Description: In R2023a and R2023b, when you assign invalid property value for FloatingPointTargetConfig  object, HDL Coder does not show error or warning message. But the invalid property value can cause downstream failure in HDL Workflow Advisor. For example, the following CLI commands do not show any warning and error message when you set invalid value?None ?to the property VendorLibrarySettings . >> fp = hdlcoder.createFloatingPointTargetConfig("NativeFloatingPoint",VendorFloatingPointLibrary="ALTERAFPFUNCTIONS")  fp =?  ? FloatingPointTargetConfig with properties:  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Library: 'NATIVEFLOATINGPOINT'  ? ? ? ? ? LibrarySettings: [1ˇÁ1 fpconfig.NFPLatencyDrivenMode]  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?IPConfig: [1ˇÁ1 hdlcoder.FloatingPointTargetConfig.IPConfig]  ? ? ? ? ? ? VendorLibrary: 'ALTERAFPFUNCTIONS'  ? ? VendorLibrarySettings: [1ˇÁ1 fpconfig.FrequencyDrivenMode]  ? ? ? ? ? ?VendorIPConfig: [1ˇÁ1 hdlcoder.FloatingPointTargetConfig.IPConfig]  >> fp.VendorLibrarySettings = 'None'  fp =?  ? FloatingPointTargetConfig with properties:  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? Library: 'NATIVEFLOATINGPOINT'  ? ? ? ? ? LibrarySettings: [1ˇÁ1 fpconfig.NFPLatencyDrivenMode]  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ?IPConfig: [1ˇÁ1 hdlcoder.FloatingPointTargetConfig.IPConfig]  ? ? ? ? ? ? VendorLibrary: 'ALTERAFPFUNCTIONS'  ? ? VendorLibrarySettings: 'None'  ? ? ? ? ? ?VendorIPConfig: [1ˇÁ1 hdlcoder.FloatingPointTargetConfig.IPConfig]    >> hdlset_param(bdroot, 'FloatingPointTargetConfiguration', fp)   After setting this invalid FloatingPointTargetConfig  object to Simulink model, the HDL Workflow Advisor can cause following failure on Step 3.2 (Generate RTL Code and Testbench) . Failed Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.  Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.  Error in hdlcoder.FloatingPointTargetConfig/serializeOutMScripts (line 288)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? vendorLibSettingStr = this.VendorLibrarySettings.serializeOutMScripts();    Error in privhdlsaveparams>setParam (line 352)  ? ? ? ? scripts = val.serializeOutMScripts();    Error in privhdlsaveparams>setModelParams (line 139)  ? ? paramSet = setParam(sys, paramName, val, fil, paramSet, false);?
Workaround: Workaround 1Open the Floating Point Configuration Paramter tab.Uncheck the Use Floating Point?checkbox to remove floating point settings from the model.Check the checkbox again and make the required settings.Workaround 2?? ? ? ? 1. Create a FloatingPointTargetConfig object.fp = hdlcoder.createFloatingPointTargetConfig("NativeFloatingPoint");? ? ? ? 2. Customize properties as needed with appropriate valuefp.LibrarySettings.LatencyStrategy = 'Min';?? ? ? ? 3. Set the created object to the Simulink model?hdlset_param(bdroot, 'FloatingPointTargetConfiguration', fp);?

Summary: Setting the Exponent property on an axes ruler to a negative integer value displays a large positive integer-valued exponent
Description: If the Exponent property of an axes ruler is set to a negative integer value, the exponent might be?displayed as a large positive integer value. This can occur in one of two ways: Opening a figure file containing an axes that has a negative integer value for one of its rulers' Exponent property. Setting the Exponent property of an axes ruler to a negative integer value inside a Live Script.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: GPU atomic operation moved inside for-loop
Description: When using GPU Coder, the generated code might be incorrect if all  the following conditions are true: The MATLAB code contains a pair of for -loops, with one nested inside the other. A GPU atomic operation such as?gpucoder.atomicAdd  is nested between the two loops. The atomic operation operates on a scalar variable.  In such situations, in the generated code, the atomic operation might be incorrectly moved inside the inner loop. For example, the following design function produces incorrect code: function [A,t] = design()A = coder.nullcopy(zeros(100));t = 0;for j = 1:100    t = gpucoder.atomicAdd(t, 1);    for i = 1:100        A(i,j) = i+j;    endendend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Parallel nest of for-loops with too many iterations
Description: When using GPU Coder, the generated code might be incorrect if all  the following conditions are true: The MATLAB code contains a group of two or more perfectly nested for -loops that becomes a GPU kernel in the generated code. The end values of the loops are known at compile-time. The product of the end values of the loops exceeds the value of intmax('uint64') .  When these conditions are met, an integer overflow occurs when computing the total number of iterations of the nest of loops, resulting in potentially incorrect output. The following example code triggers this issue: function t = design% The computation below triggers an integer overflow:% 968973220 * 49477 * 384773 = 2^64 + 4t = 0;coder.gpu.kernel;for k = 1:968973220    coder.gpu.kernel;    for j = 1:49477        coder.gpu.kernel;        for i = 1:384773            t = gpucoder.atomicAdd(t, 1);        end    endendend

Summary: Error tracing signal to source using sltrace?if specified end point is a subsystem with one or more unconnected output ports on the tracing path
Description: When you programmatically trace the source of a signal to a specified end point, the sltrace ?function issues this error if the end point is a subsystem with one or more unconnected output ports that are on the tracing path:  Invalid Simulink object handle.   For example, this command traces the source of a signal from the block startblk ?to the end point  endblk ?specified using the  Stop ?name-value argument.  g = sltrace(startblk,"source",Stop=endblk);   This command results in the error if endblk ?is a subsystem with one or more unconnected output ports. ?

Summary: Missing operational requirement in the Polyspace Code Prover traceability matrix
Description: The Polyspace Code Prover traceability matrix (matrix_to_req.xlx ) in the DO Qualification Kit does not include mapping between the test cases and these operational requirements: HLR-3-CP Check Colors Are Sound HLR-4-CP Call Graphs HLR-5-CP Call Graphs Are Sound HLR-6-1-CP Data Dictionary HLR-6-2-CP Data Accesses Referenced in Dictionaries Are Sound HLR-6-3-CP Shared Statuses Referenced in Dictionaries Are Sound HLR-13-CP Stack Usage Metrics HLR-21-CP Outputs HLR-31-*-CP Abnormal Operating Modes
Workaround: An updated traceability matrix (matrix_to_req.xlx) is attached to this bug report.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: UseFloatingPoint cannot be turned off through the command line interface (CLI).
Description: When the UseFloatingPoint  parameter is initially set to on  and the FloatingPointTargetConfiguration  parameter is assigned a non-empty value, you are unable to set the UseFloatingPoint  parameter to off  using the following CLI command: ? ? ? ?hdlset_param(bdroot, 'UseFloatingPoint', 'off')  This limitation in the CLI prevents you to disable the UseFloatingPoint  functionality for a Simulink model. The?UseFloatingPoint ?parameter can be successfully changed from?off ?to?on ?using the same CLI without any issue.?
Workaround: You can disable the floating-point configuration by accessing the Floating Point configuration parameters and unchecking the Use Floating Point?option.

Summary: Collection of signal range coverage is disabled for model mSignalRangeLimit.slx in the Simulink Coverage qualification test suite
Description: In model?mSignalRangeLimit.slx , the Constant block that provides the signal input disables collection of the signal range coverage by Simulink Coverage because the signal width exceeds the specified limit.

Summary: C Function blocks with?sizeof?operators might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash during code generation if your Simulink model contains a C Function block, which integrates the C code that meets these conditions: The code uses the sizeof  operator on a type. The type is not used in the C code.  For example, this C Code in a C Function block might cause MATLAB to crash during code generation: typedef struct NameValue_t{? char aName[56];? unsigned char aVal;} NameValue8;int a;a = sizeof(NameValue8);?

Summary: Simulink model containing a port-scoped Simulink Function block connected to a root Inport block might crash during simulation
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a Simulink model where all the following conditions are true:?  Simulink Function block Scope  parameter is set to port .? Simulink Function block is connected to a root Inport block.? The model containing the Simulink Function block is referenced in another model.?   The crash is caused by the Inport block with constant sample time creating an empty execution list during model initialization.?
Workaround: Use ArgIn and ArgOut blocks in Simulink Function blocks instead of Inport and Outport blocks.

Summary: Angular velocity computation in Spacecraft Dynamics block is incorrect when inertia tensor has nonzero off-diagonal terms
Description: Angular velocity computation in Spacecraft Dynamics block is incorrect when the inertia tensor has nonzero off-diagonal terms. For example, this inertia tensor is affected: [100 10 20;10 200 30;20 30 150]  This inertia tensor is not affected: [100 0 0;0 200 0;0 0 150]
Workaround: Do not use an inertia tensor with nonzero off-diagonal terms. Instead, use a similarity transformation to make the inertia tensor diagonal.A similarity transformation changes the new body axes to be the eigenvectors and the diagonal terms of the inertia tensor to be the eigenvalues. Calculate the eigenvectors and the diagonal matrix with the eigenvalues using the eig function:[eigenVector,diagonalInertiaTensor]=eig(inertiaTensor);
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: XLS files containing invalid UTF-8 characters read with xlsread in basic mode can cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Certain XLS files containing invalid UTF-8 characters read with xlsread  in basic  mode can cause MATLAB to crash. For example, in the following code, crash.xls ?causes a MATLAB crash because it contains invalid UTF-8 characters and is read with xlsread  in basic  mode. >> [num, txt] = xlsread("crash.xls", "", "", "basic") The resulting crash dump includes the following phrase. mxCreateString called with non-UTF-8 input xlsread  uses basic  mode by default on platforms that do not have a Microsoft Excel COM server implementation.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Checks removed from the Model Advisor in R2021b were not removed from the Simulink?Check validation test suite
Description: These checks were removed from the Model Advisor in R2021b: Check usage of equality operators in MATLAB Function blocks (mathworks.hism.himl_0009 ) Check usage of shift operations for Stateflow data (mathworks.hism.hisf_0064 )  However, the checks were still supported by the Simulink Check validation test suite.
Workaround: To prevent the test procedure from executing the tests for the checks, delete from artifact?Simulink Check - Trace Matrix?(certkitiec_slchk_tests.xlsx) the rows for these test cases:SLCHK_HI_Q074_SFSLCHK_HI_Q059

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: stencilfun with partially fixed size input and row-major layout
Description: When performing GPU code generation for MATLAB code that uses stencilfun , incorrect code might be generated if the following conditions are met:  The call to stencilfun  is performed in a row-major context (e.g. using coder.rowMajor ). The input array provided to stencilfun  is partially fixed size (i.e. one dimension is fixed in size, but another dimension is not).
Workaround: Use column-major array layout inside the calling function of stencilfun. For example, by adding the line?coder.columnMajor to the calling function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsystem code might be incorrect when the subsystem contains a masked Model block
Description: The generated code for a model that meets all these conditions might be incorrect: The model contains at least two identical instances of a subsystem that contains a Model block. The Model block references a model that defines a model argument. The referenced model is either a function-call model or a model with a virtual bus at a root input or output port. Either the referenced model defines a system mask, or the Model block is directly masked. The model argument?values passed into the two different instances of the Model block are different.  The code generator might produce one function for all instances of the subsystem, using a hard-coded value for the model argument.

Description: Invoking the MEX(C++) function created using MEX command and codegen functions fails on macOS 14(Intel Processor) with Xcode 15. This error occurs only when C++ source files are used to build MEX functions. You will see an error like the following.?? O2IjBmOPFE/ arrayProduct. mexmaci64':   dlopen(/ private/ var/ folders/ 4j/ vf0cl0jj45702svdc52dc1_r000hvf/ T/ tmp. O2IjBmOPFE/ foo. mexmaci64, 0x0006):   Library not loaded: @rpath/ libMatlabEngine. dylib ??Referenced from: <9D16451D-148D-35E1-BC6C-9DBFCB246238> ??/ private/ var/ folders/ 4j/ vf0cl0jj45702svdc52dc1_r000hvf/ T/ tmp. O2IjBmOPFE/ foo. mexmaci64 ??  Reason: tried:?  '/Applications/MATLAB_R2023b.app/bin/maci64/ libMatlabEngine. dylib'(no such file), ?? '/Applications/MATLAB_R2023b.app/bin/maci64/ . . / . . / sys/ os/ maci64/ libMatlabEngine. dylib' ??(no such file),

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code is incorrect when a Parameter Writer block writes to a model workspace variable used by a Model block and any other built-in block with tunable parameters
Description: Generated code is incorrect when a Parameter Writer block writes to a model workspace variable that is used by a Model block with storage class for the variable set to?Model default  and used by? any other built-in block with tunable parameters.

Summary: Computing the LU decomposition of a sparse matrix twice in a row on Apple Silicon might not return the same answers
Description: If you compute the LU decomposition of a sparse matrix twice in a row on an Apple Silicon machine that is running natively-built MATLAB, the answers you receive might be slightly different between the two calls. The answers are still correct within the limits of floating-point arithmetic but are not bit-for-bit identical. This affects the lu  and inv  functions, the decomposition  object, and the \  operator. This issue does not affect Intel-based MATLAB that is run via Rosetta2.?
Workaround: Restricting multi-threading of MATLAB seems to circumvent the problem. You can achieve this by doing one of the following:Call the following command after starting MATLAB:>> maxNumCompThreads(1) Start MATLAB with the option "-singleCompThread", as in:$ matlab -singleCompThreadNotice that both workarounds will drastically decrease performance of MATLAB.?

Summary: Collection of signal range coverage is disable for model mSignalRangeLimit.slx in the Simulink Coverage qualification test suite
Description: In model mSignalRangeLimit.slx , the Constant block that provides the signal input disables collection of the signal range coverage by Simulink Coverage because the signal width exceeds the specified limit.

Summary: Design Verifier compatibility check errors out if Code Generation settings from referenced configset are overridden.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier compatibility check may error out if any of Code Generation  settings from the referenced configuration set are overridden in a model. For example: Design Verifier reports the following error if the?Custom Code ?settings under Code Generation ?are overridden to specify custom headers and source files for a C Caller block:Error:Compiling model failed: C Caller block '<path_to_C_Caller_block>' requires model to have associated importable custom code. There is no custom code specified, or 'Import custom code' is disabled.  Error:Simulink Design Verifier failed to initialize: '<model_name>' is incompatible for <design_verifier_analysis_mode> with Simulink Design Verifier.?
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Missing call sites of Stateflow temporal functions
Description: Simulink PLC Coder generated code is missing call sites of Stateflow absolute time temporal logic functions after  and before? when the => syntax                for function block outputs is configured. Since R2023a  the configuration option PLC_UseCallOutputArgSyntax ?can be found under "PLC Code Generation\  Interface\Use output argument syntax in function?and function block calls ". In R2022a  and R2022b  this option is available as fUseCallOutputArgSyntax  plugin option in the custom target IDE workflow.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: False positive violation of MISRA-C:2012 rule 1.3 on function-like macros that are invoked using empty arguments in C99
Description: Polyspace? incorrectly raises a violation in C99 when a function-like macro is called with fewer than expected arguments. Invoking function-like macros with fewer arguments is not undefined behavior in C99. #define FUNC(X) Xvoid foo() {  FUNC();  // Incorrect violation - not UB in C99}
Products: Polyspace Bug Finder, Polyspace Bug Finder Server

Summary: Generated DDS executable might drop messages associated with onDataAvailable input event
Description: The executable generated from a DDS Blockset model might drop messages when all the following conditions are true: Configuration parameter Toolchain  is set to RTI Connext 6.x Project . The model contains an explicit partition scheduled to respond to events. An input port (Inport block) is connected to the explicit partition. An onDataAvailable  (on_data_available) event is configured on the input port.?  If the generated executable is run in a domain where publishers publish messages to the topic associated with the input port in quick succession, multiple data samples might be added to the subscriber's queue, but only one 'on_data_available' event might be triggered. The current implementation processes only one received data sample in the queue per event, which might result in dropped samples if multiple samples are present.

Summary: Creating or re-creating multiple audioplayer objects in a session might cause the audio to stop playing in Web App Server and MATLAB Online
Description: Creating multiple audioplayer objects or re-creating it multiple times in a single session might cause the audio to stop playing. This issue is typically encountered in Web App Server and MATLAB Online due to a browser restriction on the number of media streams that can be created per tab. For example, after the 49th iteration, the following lines of code result in no sound: audioData = load("handel.mat");for iteration = 1:55     audioplayerObj = audioplayer(audioData.y, audioData.Fs, 24);     playblocking(audioplayerObj);     clear audioplayerObj end
Workaround: Create a single instance of the audioplayer, and reuse it to play audio with the play or playblocking function.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Conditional assignments in generated code might get incorrect values during initial RAM mapping cycles
Description: When you enable MapPersistentVarsToRAM  block property, some MATLAB state variables may get mapped to RAM in hardware. The generated HDL may take multiple cycles depending on the RAM and other registers inferred from the code.? For the generated code, some conditional assignments may get an additional latency added to its input causing an assignment of 0 in the initial cycles. In this code sample, B  and reg  are declared as persistent variables:     bval = B(B_idx);    B(B_idx) = 1;    if cond        reg(:) = bval:     end    if reg(:) >= 1000       reg(:) = 1000;     end An extra delay might get introduced on 1000  in "reg(:) = 1000; " and reg  might incorrectly be set to zero instead of 1000  in the initial cycles.

Summary: HDL code generation may fail when the input dimensions of a Concatenate block are 1x1xn and the base type is complex.
Description: For Concatenate block, with complex input signals of dimension 1x1xn results in internal assertion as below; Error using hdlcoder. pirctx/ endEmissionUnexpected error during HDL Code Generation. Contact support@mathworks. com  with reproduction steps. Assertion failed with debug message:B:\ matlab\ src\ cgir_hdl\ hdl_emitters\ VHDLPIREmitter. cpp:1146:TypeChangeComp: array types have different numbers ofdimensions

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Entry-point function accepts gpuArray input and is called by another entry-point function
Description: Code generator might produce incorrect code if?all? these conditions are true: You generate code for multiple entry-point functions One of these entry-point functions (say, bar ) accepts a?gpuArray  input This function? bar  is called by another entry-point function  For example, consider the following functions?foo  and bar,  where bar ?is called inside foo : function y = foo(u)coder.gpu.kernelfun();y = bar(2 * u);endfunction y = bar(v)coder.gpu.kernelfun();y = 2 * v;end If you use the following?codegen ?command to generate a MEX for both foo  and bar , the generated MEX function mymex  might produce incorrect results. codegen -config coder.gpuConfig foo -args {ones(10)} bar -args {gpuArray(ones(10))} -o mymex
Workaround: Generate code for each entry-point separately.?For example, for the MATLAB code in the previous section, use two separate codegen commands to generate separate MEX functions foo_mex and bar_mex.?codegen -config coder.gpuConfig foo -args {ones(10)}?codegen -config coder.gpuConfig bar -args {gpuArray(ones(10))}

Summary: Code Prover analysis stuck at Software Safety Analysis Level 112 (P_C) with exception "Core.Match(nonexhaustive match failure)"
Description: When your code contains floating point management of NaN or Inf, in certain cases, the analysis might get stuck at Software Safety Analysis Level 0. This can happen when using the option -allow-non-finite-floats . You might see an error message such as the following: **** Software Safety Analysis Level 0 - 112 (P_C) exception Core.Match(nonexhaustive match failure) raised. raised at: Raised at Infos.Range_List.collapse in file "../../verifier/kernel_api/common/Infos.sml", line 1812, characters 31-36
Products: Polyspace Code Prover Server, Polyspace Code Prover

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur during Simulink Real-Time external simulations of models with parameter values that refer to an index greater than 1 in an array of structures
Description: When you run an external simulation of a Simulink Real-Time model, the simulation can generate incorrect results if the model has parameter values that refer to an index greater than 1 in an array of structures.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: for-loops with negative end values
Description: When using GPU Coder, the generated code might be incorrect if all  the following conditions are true: The MATLAB code contains a group of two or more perfectly nested for -loops that becomes a GPU kernel in the generated code. At least one of the loops in the nest has a negative end value. The product of the end values of the loops is positive.  For example, the following design function generates incorrect code: function t = design(n)% `n` is negative at runtime.t = 0;for j = 1:n    for i = 1:n        t = gpucoder.atomicAdd(t, 1);    endendend

Summary: MATLAB might stall when using thread-based pools on Windows machines with large number of cores
Description: MATLAB client might stall when you use thread-based pools on Windows machines with more than 30 cores. This issue might occur when you use: Thread-based pools created with parpool("Threads")  backgroundPool   with Parallel Computing Toolbox available  The stall might occur in the first use of parallel language on that pool. For example, the call to fetchOutputs   in this code causes MATLAB to stall:>> parpool("Threads");>> fut = parfeval(@deal, 1, 42);>> fetchOutputs(fut)
Workaround: Increase the value of the MATLAB_CPPMS_THREADLIMIT environment variable to the number of cores in your machine before you start thread-based pools on the machine.? For example, to increase the value to 32, enter this code in the MATLAB command window:>> setenv("MATLAB_CPPMS_THREADLIMIT", 32)

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unassigned fields of a nested structure conditionally defined via a function call
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: On one execution path, your MATLAB code creates structure s  containing structure x , such that structure  x  has more than one field  On different execution path, ?your MATLAB code sets s equal to the return value of function? foo  Function? foo ?returns a nested structure that has fewer fields than x   For example, consider the following MATLAB code: function y = test_struct(exp)if exp    s = struct('x', struct('f1',5,'f2',15));else    s = foo();endy = s.x.f2;endfunction out = fooout.x.f1 = 3;end In MATLAB execution, test_struct(false)  produces an error, while test_struct_mex(false) ?returns an undefined value. This issue affects MATLAB Coder and MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink.?
Workaround: If your code is affected by this bug, adding a temporary variable that saves the call result should prevent the bug from happening. In the example above, this amounts to changing    s = foo();to?    tempVar = foo(); s = tempVar;
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Using Bus Selector and Bus Creator blocks in fault models can cause errors
Description: Fault Inport and Outport blocks are unable to access bus data during some edit-time operations. Consequentially Bus Selector blocks may not specify valid bus signals in fault models.
Workaround: If you know the bus type, you can add a Signal Specification block and directly specify the bus after the Fault Inport block and before the Bus Selector block.

Summary: HDL Coder may generate incorrect error message when your model includes Selector block having bus inputs of complex types.
Description: When your model contains Selector block that has bus inputs of complex types, HDL Coder generates the following error message: Inputs and output to the Switch block should be all complex or real.
Workaround: You can employ a Bus selector or Mux blocks before the Selector inputs.?

Summary: Binaries required for Tool-Coupling FMU export are missing in the installation package
Description: Some cross platform binaries required for Tool-Coupling FMU are not installed with the MATLAB installation package. This results in a failure to export Tool-Coupling FMU.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect results might occur when a Signal Conversion block is used in between Bus Creator and Bus Selector blocks
Description: The code generator contains an optimization that detects and fuses loops with the same number of iterations. However, the optimization incorrectly fuses loops and moves statements in the fused loops when all  of these conditions are true:The model contains a Bus Creator block whose output feeds into a Signal Conversion block. The output of the Signal Conversion block feeds into a Bus Selector block. The Signal Conversion block has the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization ?parameter selected.  For the model attached in?Attachments , the code generator incorrectly fuses loops as follows:? for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {??? rtb_BusCreator.c[i] = mDynBusSignalConversion__ConstP.Constant_Value[i];??? mDynBusSignalConversion_0_Y.Outport2[i] = rtb_BusCreator.c[i];? } The correct code for the model does not fuse the loops and move the loop statements in the fused loop.? for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {??? rtb_BusCreator.c[i] = mDynBusSignalConversion__ConstP.Constant_Value[i];? }? for (i = 0; i < 5; i++) {??? mDynBusSignalConversion_0_Y.Outport2[i] = rtb_BusCreator.c[i];? }

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing when passing a multi-dimensional structured type input to a Model block that references a Simulink model
Description: When passing a multi-dimensional structured type as an input to a Model block that references another model, the code generator might generate incorrect array indexing if?all ?of the following conditions are met.?  The ArrayLayout  parameter of the models in the model hierarchy is set to Row-major .  The input to the Model block is of a structured data type and has multiple dimensions. The input is configured to preserve dimensions in the generated code. The elements from the input are extracted by using blocks such as the Selector block and then passed as an input to the Model block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: "readmsg" limited to TLOG messages with messageIDs less than or equal to 255
Description: Reading Mavlink TLOG messages by using the "readmsg" function and specifying the "MessageName" name-value argument returns empty output for messages with messageIDs greater than 255.
Workaround: Use the "readmsg" function without specifying Name-Value arguments to read TLOG messages with messageIDs greater than 255For example:?tlogReader = mavlinktlog("flight.tlog");?msgData = readmsg(tlogReader);

Summary: MATLAB might crash if MATLAB code uses fixed-point data type with a word length greater than?64
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code?from MATLAB code that uses fixed-point data type with a word length greater than 64.? The stack trace for the crash might look similar to the following:[  3] 0x00007ffa34e90391 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2024a\bin\win64\emlcoder.dll+02163601 mcos::factory::Marshaler::operator=+00596977[  4] 0x00007ffa34e4a2a9 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2024a\bin\win64\emlcoder.dll+01876649 mcos::factory::Marshaler::operator=+00310025...[  7] 0x00007ffa35056b90 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2024a\bin\win64\emlcoder.dll+04025232 MLTerminate_emlcoder+00746448...[ 15] 0x00007ffa91dcaf37 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2024a\bin\win64\libmwlxeindexing.dll+00700215 MathWorks::lxe::MatrixModuleImplementation::SubscriptedDelete+00020183...[ 19] 0x00007ffa92681d99 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2024a\bin\win64\m_lxe.dll+05578137 MathWorks::lxe::XvalueFactory::XvalueDeleter::XvalueDeleter+00326681
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 9, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: <Enum_literal> undeclared?error when building a referenced Simulink model that uses enumeration types
Description: Building generated code for a referenced model might produce an <Enum_literal> undeclared  error when all ?of the following are true: The referenced model uses an enumeration type that has DataScope  set to Exported . The underlying data type of the enumeration type is set to int8 , int16 , int32 , uint8 , uint16 , or uint32 .  The header file of the enumeration type is unset or a file that is not one of the automatically-generated files.

Summary: On Windows, switching a Git branch errors when switching modifies?the content of the current folder
Description: Switching a Git branch errors when all?of these conditions are true: Your operating system is Windows. You cd'ed into a folder in your Git repository. The switch operation modifies the files in the current folder.  Switching branches cannot delete the current folder because it is in use. An example error is: >> switchBranch(gitrepo(),"main")Error using matlab.git.GitRepository/switchBranchUnable to switch branch from MyBranch to main.Caused by:Uncommitted changes will be discarded. Stash, commit, or discard the changes before switching branches.could not rmdir 'C:/MyUser/MyRepo': The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process.
Workaround: Change the current folder to the repository working folder before you switch branches:>> cd(gitrepo().WorkingFolder); >> switchBranch(gitrepo(),"main");

Summary: Generating code from a model might result in an "(isCompliantArgumentName(argName))" assertion message
Description: Generating code from a model might results in an?"(isCompliantArgumentName(argName))" ?assertion message. The assertion is random but generally occurs with S-Function Blocks inlined using TLC.
Workaround: Can be worked around by removing the following line from the system target TLC file."%assign PreCodeGenExecCompliant = 1"?
Fixed In: R2023b Update 4, R2023b Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect result when Function Caller blocks used in multiple different Reset Function blocks or Reinitialize Function blocks
Description: ?A Simulink function might?execute twice and generate incorrect results when all the following conditions are met: The Simulink function is called by Function Caller blocks that are inside multiple different Reset Function blocks or Reinitialize Function blocks. Show model reset ports ?or?Show model reinitialize?ports? option ?is selected for the Model block that references the model containing the Simulink function and the Reset Function or the Reinitialize Function blocks. Event ports related to the reset or reinitialize events are triggered during simulation.?

Summary: MATLAB user interface shows some text in Chinese when Chinese is not selected as the primary language
Description: When Chinese is selected as the secondary language of your operating system, the MATLAB user interface shows some text in Chinese.
Workaround: In the OS settings, remove Chinese as a secondary language.?

Summary: "The 2008 geopotential model is not available on the MATLAB path" error
Description: The Add-On Explorer fails to properly install the Geoid Data for Aerospace Toolbox. As a result, when you call the geoidheight  function with EGM2008  as the input argument, MATLAB returns the following error after installation: The?2008 geopotential model is not available on the MATLAB path, you might need to download it using the aeroDataPackage function.

Summary: Error might occur when building generated code containing enums with prefix 'enum_'
Description: The code generator might produce errors while building the code generated from a MATLAB function that meets these conditions:The MATLAB function contains an enum type variable whose name contains the 'enum_'  prefix.  The function contains another enum type variable with the same name without the 'enum_'  prefix.   For example, consider this MATLAB code.? ?function y = fcn(u)  ? ?y = enum_ABC.Three;  ? ? ?if u == ABC.Two  ? ?y = enum_ABC.Four;  ? ? ? end  ? ? ? ?end  The code contains two enums?enum_ABC  and ABC . The generated code for?ABC ?is: ?  ? ?#ifndef enum_ABC  ? ?#define enum_ABC  ? ? ?enum ABC  ? ? ? ?{ One = 1,  / * Default value */ ??  ?? ? ? ? Two};  ? ? ? ??#endif / * enum_ABC?*/ ?The code contains a?#define ?guard for enum ABC . The guard name starts with 'enum_' ?prefix and is followed by?ABC ,?which? conflicts with the name of the other enum?enum_ABC . Because of this conflict, the code generator cannot build?the generated code and issues an error.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder may generate incorrect code if a virtual bus with a varying element order is connected within model references
Description: HDL Coder may produce inaccurate code when dealing with virtual buses in which the element order differs. This issue arises when the virtual bus is connected to a model reference, especially when the sub-model relies on the In/Out bus element ports, and the order of bus elements does not match.

Summary: Warning displays in the Command Window after clicking the?Request Support?button on the MATLAB Home  tab
Description: When the Request Support ?button on the MATLAB Home ?tab is clicked, the Command Window displays a?StAXDialectDetector  warning. This warning does not prevent submitting a support request.

Summary: HDL code generation throws an internal error when the Variant Subsystem has trigger or enable ports.
Description: When you add a trigger or enable port inside the Variant Subsystem, HDL code generation throws the following internal error:  Dot indexing is not supported for variables of this type.

Summary: Error?Arrays have incompatible sizes for this operation?when trying to clear change issue
Description: After you author or import requirements and create links between those requirements and the design elements, Requirements Toolbox can detect when the requirements, Simulink test objects or lines of MATLAB code change and indicates that the link has a change issue. You can then clear or resolve change issues.When you try to clear a change issue by using the Requirements Editor or by using the API, you might see this error:?Arrays have incompatible sizes for this operation . When this error occurs, the change issue is not cleared.

Summary: TLC error when using the S-Function target to generate code
Description: When generating code for a model that uses the S-Function target, the code generator might return an error similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\prm_constant.tlc Line: 902 Column: 28  The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier  Main program:  ==> [00] C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2018b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\prm_constant.tlc:SLibCacheUnstructuredConstantPrmBuffers(902)?
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink Function block inside a Reset Function block or a Reinitialize Function block might execute twice
Description: Simulink Function block inside a Reset Function block or a Reinitialize Function block?might execute twice leading to a wrong answer when all the following conditions are met:A Function Caller block that is placed inside the Reset Function block or the Reinitialize Function block calls the Simulink function. The Function Caller block is inside a referenced model. The Reset Function block or Reinitialize Function block that contains the Function Caller block is also in a referenced model. Show?model reset ports  ?parameter is selected for the Model block that references?the model containing the Simulink Function block.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during model update phase of model containing Math Reciprocal block with Algorithm Method set to Newton-Raphson
Description: MATLAB might crash during model update phase of a model if all of the following conditions are true: Math Function block?Function ?is set to reciprocal .  Math Function block Algorithm Method ?is set to Newton-Raphson . Output of Math Function block is input to an Outport block. Outport block data type is specified explicitly.?
Workaround: Set the Outport block?Data type parameter to inherit data types, such as Inherit: auto.?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function in a behavior model might not run when invoked from a server port during architecture model?simulation
Description: System Composer might incorrectly propagate a constant sample time to a server port when all of the following are true: The model uses the Software Architecture template. The architecture model contains referenced architectures. A referenced architecture contains a server port.  In this situation, a function in a behavior model might not run when invoked.

Summary: Accelerator or rapid accelerator simulation might error or hang for models that contain Integrator blocks with multirate inputs
Description: Accelerator and rapid accelerator simulations incorrectly handle certain zero crossings for Integrator blocks that satisfy all of these conditions: The input signal to integrate is a multirate vector. The first element in the multirate vector signal has a discrete sample time. The Integrator block is configured to limit the output signal value within a specified range. Zero-crossing detection is enabled for both the model and the Integrator block.?  If the simulation uses a variable-step solver, the software might issue an error related to zero-crossing detection or appear to hang.  If the simulation uses a fixed-step solver, simulation might slow down and the solver might miss zero crossings.
Workaround: Replace affected Integrator blocks with multiple Integrator blocks that each integrate a scalar input signal.?

Summary: Generated code might contain MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violations
Description: The generated code might contain a MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violation for a model that meets all these conditions: The model contains Unit Delay blocks connected in series.? The states of the Unit Delay blocks use a storage class other than Auto  or GetSet . The model configuration parameter Optimize global data access  is set to Minimize global data access .  The MISRA C:2012 Rule 2.2 violation occurs because of the presence of dead code in the generated code.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code might be incorrect if Inport blocks inside referenced models use a storage class other than Auto
Description: The generated code might be incorrect for a Simulink model that meets all  of these conditions:The model contains a referenced model. At least one Inport block inside the referenced model uses a storage class other than Auto .  The generated code might contain uninitialized local variables which are accessed or read in code.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when creating code mappings for Simulink models with In Bus Element blocks
Description: MATLAB might crash when creating code mappings for a Simulink model that contains one or both of the following block configurations in the top model:? An In Bus Element  block that emits a virtual bus. An In Bus Element  block that emits a signal that is owned by a non-virtual bus.   The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [? 0] 0x00007ffa4b208bb3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00035763 foundation::core::diag::thread_context::unspecified_bool+00000051[? 1] 0x00007ffa4b207188 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00029064 foundation::core::diag::stacktrace_base::capture+00000024[? 2] 0x00007ffa4b20b43e C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00046142 foundation::core::diag::symbols::getSymbolAddress+00009854[? 3] 0x00007ffa4b20e9d4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\libmwfl.dll+00059860 foundation::core::diag::is_terminate_message_enabled+00000916[? 4] 0x00007ffa20c2185f C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcr.dll+00858207 mcr::shutdown::startWatchdog+00033135[? 5] 0x00007ffa20c21045 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcr.dll+00856133 mcr::shutdown::startWatchdog+00031061[? 6] 0x00007ffa20c1cde3 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcr.dll+00839139 mcr::shutdown::startWatchdog+00014067[? 7] 0x00007ffa20c235ee C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\mcr.dll+00865774 mnSetSegvServiceUrl+00000190[? 8] 0x00007ffa55146bf4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\VCRUNTIME140.dll+00027636 purecall+00000020[? 9] 0x00007ff9b610d50b C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\sl_autosar.dll+01365259 autosar::composition::isCompositionBlock+00126827[ 10] 0x00007ff9eb87e89d C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\libmwsl_services_mi.dll+00059549 sl::mi::PropInfo::getValue+00000029[ 11] 0x00007ff9eb52c941 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2022b\bin\win64\sl_loadsave.dll+01493313 slsWriteStandaloneObject+00001841?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: fixDiv, floorDiv, ceilDiv, nearestDiv, and fixed.system.RoundToMultiple return limited precision result
Description: For the functions?fixDiv ,? floorDiv ,? ceilDiv , and? nearestDiv , when the third argument is a value less than 1 , the precision of the returned result is limited to 21 bits in simulation and generated code. For example, when calculating the binary expansion of 1/10 to 50 bits, all values after the 22nd bit are incorrectly returned as 0. >> bin(floorDiv(fi(1), fi(10), pow2(-50)))ans =??? '0011001100110011001110000000000000000000000000000'    ?This issue is also present with the System object fixed.system.RoundToMultiple . An equivalent calculation is shown below:  >> obj = fixed.system.RoundToMultiple('Numerator', fi(1), ...'Denominator', fi(10), ...'Multiple', pow2(-50), ...'RoundingMethod', 'Floor');>> bin(step(obj, fi(1)))ans =??? '0011001100110011001110000000000000000000000000000'

Summary: Index exceeds number of array elements error when computing ceilDiv, fixDiv, floorDiv, or nearestDiv with m = 2^-128
Description: When computing the functions ceilDiv, fixDiv, floorDiv,  or nearestDiv  using the optional third argument m, an index out of bounds error is thrown when its value is equal to 2^-128, as in the example below: >> x = fi(1);>> d = fi(10);>> m = pow2(-128)>> nearestDiv(x,d,m)m =?? 2.9387e-39Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 256.

Summary: Stateflow charts containing a Simulink-based state that contains chart with an atomic box might crash during simulation
Description: MATLAB might crash when you run a Simulink model where all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Stateflow Chart The Stateflow chart contains a Simulink-based state that contains another Chart inside it? This inner Stateflow Chart contains an atomic box  The generated stack trace contains lines similar to:[  0] 0x00007ffb73288206 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023a\bin\win64\sf_runtime.dll+01278470 sf_is_jit_enabled+00000006  [  1] 0x00007ffb7323fad4 C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023a\bin\win64\sf_runtime.dll+00981716 sf_call_atomic_subchart_auto_fcn+00000036 ?

Summary: FMU Export fails when run in a project with code folder set
Description: FMU does not get copied into save directory, if custom setting for save directory and code generation directory are different.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Terminate Function block might give wrong answer in rapid accelerator mode
Description: Terminate Function block gives wrong answer in rapid accelerator mode when all the following conditions occur: The Terminate Function block calls a Data Store Read block which reads data from a Data Store Memory block. The data values change when the Terminate Function block executes. A Data Store Write block writes the updated data to the same Data Store Memory block. The data store updates twice. The Terminate Function block is inside a model that has multiple execution rates. The model containing the Terminate Function block?is referenced by another model that simulates in rapid accelerator mode.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Assertion error when generating code for MATLAB function containing consequential while loops, which can be infinite once entered
Description: Code generation for MATLAB code that contains a while  loop with a consequential body, which can be an infinite loop once entered, might produce an?assertion error. For example, consider this MATLAB code:? function y = foo(x)  ? ? y = 0; ? ? ? while x == 1  ? ? ? ? y = y + 1;  ? ? end  end   The while  loop in the function foo  is infinite once entered because the value of x  does not change in the loop. The code generation for the MATLAB code might result in a crash with the following message:  Assertion at B:\matlab\src\cg_ir\region\PstImpl.cpp line 822: In computeEnclosingRegions: Pushing null enclosing Region:
Workaround: Change the while loop condition to true,?and wrap the while loop in an if statement.For example, the MATLAB function foo?can be converted to this:?function y = foo(x)? ? y = 0;? ? if x == 1?? ? ? ? while true? ? ? ? ? ? y = y + 1;? ? ? ? end? ? end?end??
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Bug Finder ends compilation analysis with "out of memory" error
Description: If you analyze a large number of source files using Polyspace Bug Finder, the analysis can abort with the following error message:  Verifying myfile.c (XXXX/YYYY) [C]polyspace: sqlite error:unknown:B:\matlab\polyspace\src\verifier\databases_merger\merger\SQLSourcesMerger.cpp:181: rc=7 (expected rc=101) out of memorypolyspace: sqlite error:unknown:B:\matlab\polyspace\src\shared\persistence\database.cpp:76: rc=7 (expected rc=0) out of memory  This issue occurs if you analyze thousands of source files together.

Summary: Code generation for a referenced model produces error "Unexpected sigKind outputAddr"
Description: Generating code for a referenced model?produces an "Unexpected sigKind outputAddr " error under the following conditions:The model contains a Simulink Function block. The Simulink Function?block contains a Message Send block or a Stateflow chart that sends a message. The Message Send?block or Stateflow chart is connected directly to an output port of the Simulink Function block.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Generated C++ class code might be uncompilable for models configured to use fixed-point data types
Description: C++ code generation might produce uncompilable code for a model under the following conditions:The model has code interface packaging set to generate C++ class code. The model contains Inport or Outport blocks configured with fixed-point data types.  Under these conditions, the generated code contains declarations for methods ModelExternalInputInit  and ModelExternalOutputInit , but it does not contain the corresponding method definitions.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: gitinit errors when initializing a bare Git repository
Description: Creating a bare repository using the gitinit  function throws the following error: >> repo = gitinit("myfolder",Bare=true)Error using matlab.internal.git.GitRepository/getWorkingFolderThe repository "C:\myWorkSpace\myfolder" is bare. Bare repositories do not have working directories. Although the gitinit  function successfully creates a bare Git repository, the error occurs when the function attempts to refresh the source control status displayed in the MATLAB Desktop.
Workaround: You can ignore this error.The gitinit function successfully creates a bare Git repository. To refresh the source control status, navigate out and into the repository again.

Summary: MATLAB for macOS (Apple Silicon) might crash when using an unsupported JRE
Description: For MATLAB for macOS (Apple Silicon), MathWorks only supports the Java 8 JRE included with Amazon Corretto 8. For more information, see?MATLAB on Apple Silicon Macs .?When a different JRE is installed, Native Apple silicon MATLAB might load the unsupported JRE and crash on Apple Silicon. MATLAB for macOS (Intel Processor) is not affected by this bug.
Workaround: 1. Install the MathWorks supported JRE -? Java 8 JRE included with Amazon Corretto 8. To find the location of this installation, in a terminal type:/usr/libexec/java_home -V -f -a arm64 -v 1.8?2. Configure MATLAB to use the supported JRE by invoking matlab_jenv from a terminal. For the example, this command configures MATLAB R2023b to use Amazon Corretto 8 installed in the default location:/Applications/MATLAB_R2023b.app/bin/matlab_jenv /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/amazon-corretto-8.jdk/Contents/Home/jre/?

Summary: Apps containing a list box component with an empty value cannot be opened in App Designer
Description: If you create a list box in an App Designer app and set the? Value  property of the list box to empty using the Component Browser, the app can no longer be opened in App Designer. App Designer displays a message:  "One or more components in your file have experienced an internal error.? Opening this file will remove these components and cause loss of functionality or errors."
Workaround: Instead of setting the Value property of the list box using the Component Browser or the canvas, set the property using the app StartupFcn callback.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Function Caller block with array type inputs might result in incorrect code for AUTOSAR adaptive model
Description: Incorrect code is generated for an AUTOSAR model if all the following conditions are met.The model is configured for system target file autosar_adaptive.tlc . The model contains a Function Caller block.  The Function Caller block is mapped to an adaptive service method. The Function Caller block has multiple array type inputs of the same size.  Under these conditions, the generated code for the function call might have reused input variables. This can lead to incorrect execution behavior.
