Summary: Simulink models that use variable-size signals with model reference might produce a silent wrong answer
Description: Simulating a model might result in a silent wrong answer when all these conditions apply: A nonvirtual bus containing at least one variable-size signal is input to a Model block. The Model block is configured to simulate in accelerator mode. The Model block has only input ports and no output ports. The simulation logs data for the variable-size signal in the bus.
Workaround: Configure the Model block to simulate in normal mode. Then, simulate the top model in normal mode.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator generates During execution sender service call inside Stateflow local function instead of in entry-point function
Description: The code generator might generate a sender service call inside a Stateflow local function instead of in an entry-point function when?all ?of the following are true: A root outport is mapped to a sender service that uses the During execution communication method. That root outport is driven by a graphical function. That graphical function is called by a Stateflow Chart.  For example, consider a top model,?mld , that includes a Stateflow Chart, Emitter , which calls a graphical function,?foo . foo  sends data to the root outport.?The code generator produces this code for the model: static void mdl_foo(void)  {  ??? set_mdl_step_Outport1(3.0);  }  void mdl_step(void){  ??? int32_T idx;  ??? for (idx = 0; idx < 5; idx++) {  ??????? mdl_foo();  ??? }  } ?         The generated entry-point function, mdl_step , does not set the root outport value via a sender service. Instead, the generated code sets the root outport value inside graphical function.?This violates the During execution  communication method because the mdl_step  function calls mdl_foo  multiple times from the for- loop.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generator does not generate sender service call for root outport?that is used inside a deeply nested Initialize Function or Terminate Function subsystem block
Description: The code generator does not generate a sender service call for a root outport when?all ?of the following are true: The root outport is mapped to a sender service. The root outport is used inside an Initialize Function or Terminate Function subsystem block. That subsystem is nested at least 2 layers under the top model.  For example, consider a top model,?top , that includes a subsystem, mid , which also nests a subsystem,?bot . The?bot ?subsystem has an initialize function,?initialize . A root outport, Out1 , is connected to?initialize . The code generator produces this code for the model:?  void?bot_initialize(void)?{?? top_DW.Out1 = 2*get_In1(); }  ?  void?mid_initialize(void)?{?? bot_initialize(); }?  ?  void top_initialize(void){??mid_initialize(); }?   The generated function,? top_initialize , does not send out the variable top_DW.Out1  via a sender service call. The generated function should assign this value by using an access function, set_Out1(top_DW.Out1) .

Summary: HDL code generation for the Compare To Constant block throws an error when an expression is provided in the 'Constant value' field.
Description: There is an unmeaningful error thrown during HDL code generation of the Compare To Constant  block on providing expression to the 'Constant value' field. The error says - "Inputs to the fi constructor cannot be of class Simulink.data.Expression."

Summary: HDL code generation may fail when using the model reference as the DUT and the referenced model has no ports.
Description: HDL code generation may fail when using the model reference as the DUT and the referenced model has no input or output ports.? The HDL code generation stops with the following unexpected error message Index exceeds the number of array elements. Index must not exceed 0.  Error in slhdlcoder.Hdlcoder/nonTopDutDriver?
Workaround: Use a DUT that must have at least one output port; otherwise, HDL Coder will interpret it as a non-functional (dead or unused) subsystem, resulting in the generation of an empty module code and the unexpected error.

Summary: Statement coverage is incorrect for a for-loop whose body does not run
Description: If your source code includes a for -loop whose body does not run, a statement coverage analysis incorrectly reports that the for  statement was not executed even if the your tests exercised the loop. For example, assume that the following source and test code exist in the?source.m ?and?testSource.m ?files in your current folder. The for -loop in?source.m ?does not run because the loop index is empty. However, the test in?testSource.m  exercises the loop and runs the for  statement once. function sourcefor i = 1:0    disp(i)endendclassdef testSource < matlab.unittest.TestCase    methods (Test)        function test1(~)            source        end    endend If you generate a code coverage report for the source code in?source.m , the report mistakenly indicates that the for  statement was not executed by the test. >> runtests("testSource.m","ReportCoverageFor","source.m");

Summary: Variable Scaling Analyzer shows no output for some Data Import/Export settings
Description: The Variable Scaling Analyzer shows no output when running a model with the following model configuration settings:? Dataset signal format  is set to?timetable .? Log Dataset data to file ?is on .
Workaround: When using the Variable Scaling Analyzer, make sure that both?the following settings are set appropriately:Set the dataset signal format to timeseries. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the Data Import/Export tab, under?Advanced parameters, set Dataset signal format to?timeseries. You can also use?set_param(model,'DatasetSignalFormat','timeseries').?Turn logging to file off. In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, on the?Data Import/Export?tab, clear the Log Dataset data to file?check box. You can also use?set_param(model,'LoggingToFile','off').
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Summary validation reports reference an artifact not available in the IEC Certification Kit
Description: Summary validation reports (certkitiec_<product>_validationreport.html ), which provide the results of executing the validation tests, reference document Test Cases and Procedures . In the IEC Certification Kit, this information was available in the User's Guide. Starting in R2022b, topic "Execute Tool Test Cases and Procedures" was removed from the User's Guide.
Workaround: The information in "Execute Tool Test Cases and Procedures" is now available in these tool-specific artifacts:Tool Operational Requirements (mathworks_<product>_tor)Execute Validation Tests and Review Test Results for <Product> (certkitiec_<product>_run.mlx)

Summary: Unable to?install Data Acquisition Toolbox Support Package for Measurement Computing Hardware
Description: When you try to install the?Data Acquisition Toolbox Support Package for Measurement Computing Hardware from the Add-on Explorer, the installation fails because of a change in location of driver resources. An error dialog box includes the message "Failed to download the third-party software: MCCUniversalLibrary."
Workaround: Use the following steps to manually install the necessary device driver.Save this installer file to your downloads location:https://files.digilent.com/archive/MCCDAQ_CD/Archive_6.60/mccdaq.exeExecute the downloaded file to install the driver.

Summary: False positive violation of CERT-C EXP46-C on parenthesized expression
Description: Polyspace raises an incorrect violation of CERT-C EXP46-C when you use parenthesized expressions to indicate a bitwise operation. For example, in the following code, Polyspace reports a false positive violation even though the bitwise &  operates on a parenthesized expression: int A, B, C;     if (A & (B == C))   // false positive violation{   //...}
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Simulink fails to report consistent results when Rate LimiterDynamic block has continuous input signal
Description: The Rate Limiter Dynamic block does not error out with a continuous input signal and a variable-step solver. These configurations cause inconsistent sampling and the caching of outdated values, which can cause unexpected results.?
Workaround: To convert the input signal from continuous time to discrete-time, add a Zero-Order Hold block between the input signal and the Rate Limiter Dynamic block.

Summary: createInputDataset function fails when it cannot resolve variables in model workspace
Description: The  createInputDataset ?function fails when it cannot resolve variables, such as?Simulink Parameters , that are? stored in the base workspace. The function returns these parameters as empty and might return errors at the command line, for example: Incorrect number or types of inputs or outputs for function isfinite. Error in Simulink. sta. editor. setModelLoggingParameters (line 77)  ????if isempty(evaluatedFixedStep) || ~isfinite(evaluatedFixedStep) || ~isscalar( evaluatedFixedStep )  Error in createInputDataset (line 167)Simulink. sta. editor. setModelLoggingParameters( originalModel,  modelToGenerate);

Summary: Wrong samples returned when capturing large amounts of data using multiple antennas
Description: Your capture data might contain wrong samples under the following conditions: You define multiple antennas for your basebandReceiver  object. You call the capture  function with a data length that exceeds the size of the onboard memory buffer size. You call the capture  function again, without clearing the object.  Under these conditions, the first samples in your captured data may contain samples from the previous capture.
Workaround: Clear the basebandReceiver object between captures. For example:bbrx = basebandReceiver("MyRadio");?bbrx.Antennas = ["RF0:RX2","RF1:RX2"] ;[data1,timestamp,droppedSamples] = capture(bbrx,seconds(5));% Clear to ensure no old samples for next capture?clear bbrx?% Recreate object for second capture?bbrx = basebandReceiver("MyRadio");?bbrx.Antennas = ["RF0:RX2","RF1:RX2"] ;[data2,timestamp,droppedSamples] = capture(bbrx,seconds(5));?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect code generation for a MATLAB Function block that contains vector indexing code
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect code for a model that contains a MATLAB Function block that uses vector indexing. For example, consider a simple model that contains a MATLAB Function block that contains this MATLAB code. function P12 = set0001_funToCall(IA4) [P12] = set0001_funOnPath2(IA4);  endfunction P12 = set0001_funOnPath2(IA4) coder.inline('never');P8 = int32(ones(2));P8(:) = transpose(IA4(1,2:1:5));P12 = P8(2,2);  end The MATLAB Function block receives input from an Inport block and outputs to an Outport block. The Inport block outputs a 4-by-5 matrix signal.? The code generator incorrectly produces an empty step function. void model_step()?   {  }?  The code generator should produce code similar to the following.  double??set0001_funOnPath2(double IA4[20])  {  ? return IA4[16];?  }?  void model_step()?  {  ? Out1 = set0001_funOnPath2(In1);  }?
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: LCC compiler bug might produce unexpected SIL simulation results
Description: If the generated C code defines and uses unsigned long long  variables and your selected compiler is LCC, then a compiler bug might cause software-in-the-loop (SIL) simulations to produce unexpected results.
Workaround: ?Use another compiler:Install a?supported compiler.To select the compiler, in the MATLAB Command Window, run mex -setup?and follow the instructions.

Summary: Summary qualification reports reference an artifact not available in the DO Qualification Kit
Description: Summary qualification reports (qualkitdo_<product>_qualificationnreport.html ), which provides the results of executing the qualification tests, reference artifact?Test Cases and Procedures ?(qualkitdo_<product>_tcp ). Starting in R2023a, the tool-specific?Test Cases and Procedures ?artifact was removed from the DO Qualification Kit.
Workaround: The content of the?Test Cases and Procedures?artifact is available in these tool-specific artifacts:Tool Operational Requirements?(mathworks_<product>_tor)Execute Qualification Tests and Review Test Results for <Product>?(qualkitdo_<product>_run.mlx)

Summary: Using?SharingFactor?with reusable subsystems might force design delays in the subsystem to be mapped to RAM-based shift registers
Description: When you share multiple instances of a reusable subsystem, design delays, such as Unit Delay and Integer Delay blocks, might map to RAM even if the UseRAM  option on the block is set to off .? To check whether RAM resources exist in the generated code, and if unrequested RAM mapping occurs for regions of the model that have sharing requests, see the HDL Code Resource Report.
Workaround: To avoid the forced RAM mapping of delays, use either one of these settings:Increase the RAMMappingThreshold on the modelTurn on DistributedPipelining?for the subsystems being shared

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block with different numbers of input and output dimensions might use incorrect padded values
Description: As the top block of the reproduction model attached to this bug report shows, the Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block can produce incorrect output when all ?of these conditions are met: The subsystem has different numbers of input and output dimensions (for example, 3-dimensional input and 2-dimensional output). The output has a dimension that has a size of 1 (for example, the output is a 1-by-10 matrix) The Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block has an output size and region of interest that require the subsystem to use padded values from outside the input matrix  The Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block incorrectly reads values from outside the input matrix instead of applying the padding method that you configure with the Padding method ?block parameter. This produces incorrect output.
Workaround: 1. Eliminate the output dimensions of size 1 by pre-padding the input matrix before the Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block receives the input by using one of these methods:To replicate the behavior of the Constant?padding method, use a Pad block.?To replicate other padding methods for 2-dimensional and 3-dimensional matrices, use an Image Pad block.For input matrices with more than 3 dimensions, use another Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block to pre-pad the input. The pre-padding Neighborhood Processing Subsystem block should contain a Selector block that selects the center pixel of each neighborhood. Set the Output size?parameter for the Neighborhood block inside the pre-padding subsystem?to Full.2. The above pre-padding methods produce an output matrix that contains the correct output matrix surrounded by extraneous values. Use a Selector block to select the desired output region from the output matrix.?

Summary: trainnet errors for multi-output networks when using the L-BFGS solver
Description: The trainnet ?function errors for multi-output networks when using the L-BFGS solver. The software throws this error: ?Error using deep.internal.train.Trainer/train  Assigning to 2 elements using a simple assignment statement is not supported. Consider using comma-separated list assignment.    Error in deep.internal.train.trainnet (line 51)  net = train(trainer, net, mbq);    Error in trainnet (line 91)  ? ? [net,info] = deep.internal.train.trainnet(mbq, net, loss, options, ...?
Workaround: Train the multi-output network using a custom training loop and update the learnable parameters using the lbfgsupdate?function.?

Summary: Build error for C2000 hardware board with only?Texas Instruments C2000Ware?setup
Description: If only?Texas Instruments C2000Ware dependent C2000 hardware board?has been configured using third-party tool setup for C2000 Microcontroller Blockset (MATLAB command: c2000setup), you might experience the build failures when you try to generate code and build the executable from the Simulink model. The error you will see will be as below:"The path does not exist, error code: 0".
Workaround: 1. Install the Texas Instruments controlSUITE software.2. Run the?third-party tool setup for C2000 Microcontroller Blockset?and select?Texas Instruments controlSUITE dependent C2000 hardware board such as?TI Piccolo F2803x or TI Piccolo F2806x?even though you do not use them.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: SIMD code for loops with negative stride involving complex types
Description: For some MATLAB coding or Simulink modeling patterns, the code generator incorrectly applies SIMD instructions to loops containing negative stride and computations with complex types, which leads to different numerical results. Consider the following MATLAB code in a MATLAB Function block.?in1 ?is complex data of type  single  and the model uses the instruction set SSE2:  function out = fcn(in1, in2)  ? ? out = zeros(size(in1), 'like', in1);  ? ? for i = 1:numel(in1)  ? ? ? ? out(i) = in1(numel(in1)-i+1) .* in2;  ? ? end  end  The code contains a loop with negative stride, where with the increase of i ,  the index of in1 , decreases in the computation pattern?in1(numel(in1)-i+1) . With this code pattern, t he code generator incorrectly uses SIMD instructions, which affects the numerical results. The code generator generates this C code for the MATLAB Function block:  ? ? for (i = 0; i <= 78; i += 2) {  ? ? ? ? tmp = _mm_mul_ps(_mm_loadu_ps((const real32_T *)&rtU.in1[79 - i]), _mm_set1_ps(30.0F));  ? ? ? ? _mm_storeu_ps((real32_T *)&rtY.out[i], tmp);  ? ? }
Workaround: 1. Open the Simulink Coder? app or the Embedded Coder app.2. Click Settings > Hardware Implementation.3. On the Optimization pane, for the Leverage target hardware instruction set extensions parameter, select 'None' to stop generating SIMD code.

Summary: Unnecessary rebuild for models that contain variant blocks
Description: For models that contain variant blocks, the code generator might perform an unnecessary rebuild when all of the following conditions are true:The model is configured to generate code for an ERT-based target. The model contains variant blocks with the Variant activation?time  parameter set to code compile.  Those variant blocks define their variant choices using Simulink.Parameter  objects with a custom storage class of macro type. For example,Define , ImportedDefine , CompilerFlag , or a user-defined storage class of macro type.?  To reproduce this issue:With the top model open, generate code. Close and then reopen the model. Without making any changes to the model,?generate code again. The code generator incorrectly performs an incremental rebuild of the model and?any child models that also meet these conditions.
Workaround: Use a Simulink.VariantControl object as a variant control variable. For example:V = Simulink.VariantControl;V.Value = 1;V.ActivationTime = 'code compile';Use this in the variant control expression of a variant block. For example: V == 1The value of the?Simulink.VariantControl?object can also be a Simulink.Parameter?object with custom storage class of macro type. For example,V.Value = Simulink.Parameter(int32(1))With this workaround, the code generator reports a warning that the variant block is not configured with VariantActivationTime set to inherit from Simulink.VariantControl, but code generation is not impacted.For other workaround options, you can modify the variant control variables of the variant blocks to use one of these:?An enum of type IntEnumTypeA MATLAB variable

Summary: Horizontal zoom or autoscale on Spectrum Analyzer block changes spectral content if input is fixed-point
Description: Spectrum Analyzer block spectral content might change if the horizontal zoom or autoscale was performed after the Simulink model stops running and if the input provied to the scope is of fixed-point typ.e
Workaround: Insert a "Data Type Conversion" block between the scope and the fixed-point inputs. Set the Output data type to 'double'.

Summary: Simulating multiple referenced model instances in normal mode might show incorrect port value labels
Description: When debugging the visible model simulation with port value labels displayed, the port label values might incorrectly display values from a non-visible instance instead of the visible instance. Incorrect port value labels might occur when:?  The model with multi-instance model reference is simulated in normal mode There is a Bus Assignment block upstream of the referenced model
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Indexed assignment with multiple indices
Description: MATLAB supports indexed assignments where the shape of the right-hand side and left-hand side are not identical. For example, x(1:5) = zeros(5,1);  is permitted, though the shapes are different (1x5  vs 5x1 ).  In certain situations, such MATLAB coding patterns might cause incorrect code generation. This happens if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: The left-hand side indexing has at least 2 indices: x(a,b,...) = RHS  The shapes of the left-hand side and right-hand side are not the same, but are compatible for indexed assignment in MATLAB  In such situations, the generated code might: Not write to some elements of the left-hand side, and?  Write multiple elements of the right-hand side into the same element in the left-hand side.  For example, with a runtime-valued M  with value 1, and a runtime-valued?N , x(1:N,1:M) = 1:N;  assigns a?1x? ?RHS into a??x? ?LHS during code generation, which becomes a?1xN  RHS into a Nx1  LHS at runtime. For this code pattern, the generated code might incorrectly only write to the first element of x .
Workaround: Explicitly reshape the right-hand side to match the shape of the left-hand side.For example, replacex(1:N,1:M) = 1:N;withx(1:N,1:M) = reshape(1:N, N, M);

Summary: Parameter overrides with array data might not be saved in test file and display incorrectly in Test Manager
Description: Test cases that contain parameter override array data with greater than ten elements might result in the parameter override data not being saved in the test file. Additionally, these parameter values might be displayed as Derived from file ?in the Test Manager.??

Summary: Library filters disabled in the DO-178C example when using?sl_refresh_customizations
Description: Access to the Helicopter Flight Control example with the command openExample('do_qualkit/DOQualificationProjectExample')  triggers a filter which enables only the (qualified) Simulink and DO-178C/DO-331 Primitive libraries.? When initially running command sl_refresh_customizations , the filter is incorrectly disabled and all libraries are available for use.? Only when rerunning sl_refresh_customizations  is the filter reapplied.? A similar problem is observed in the DO-178C project template as described here .

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multiple MATLAB Function blocks using MATLAB Function architecture and SubsystemReuse set to 'Atomic and Virtual' might generate incorrect HDL code
Description: HDL code generation may generate incorrect code or crash MATLAB when generating code for a model with both  these conditions: The model contains multiple identical MATLAB Function blocks with Architecture  property set to MATLAB Function . Model configuration parameter Code Reuse  (SubsystemReuse ) is set to 'Atomic and Virtual '.

Summary: Signal Editor block signal properties do not save correctly if?Active Signal?parameter is changed before clicking?Apply
Description: The Active Signal ?parameter of the Signal Editor block determines which signal is selected for signal property modification. If the Apply  button is not clicked before the Active Signal ?is changed, the modified signal properties might not save correctly.
Workaround: After changing a signal property, click the Apply?button before changing the Active Signal?parameter value.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Job creation or submission on MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster might fail when attaching multiple files
Description: When creating or submitting a job to a MATLAB Job Scheduler?cluster with a large number of attached files, the creation or submission might fail with the error:  Failed to update job in the job manager database ? The issue occurs with files you explicitly attach with the AttachedFiles ?argument to the batch,?createJob?or createCommunicatingJob  functions, or the AttachedFiles  property on the parallel.Job  object, or files MATLAB automatically attaches. The issue is not caused by the size of the attached files, simply by the number.
Workaround: Instead of attaching a large number of files, place all files you want to attach into a single folder, and attach just this single folder.

Summary: Error when generating C++ class interface code from a model reference hierarchy using parallel builds
Description: The code generation process may stop with and error out for a model hiearchy that meets these conditions: The model hierarchy is built in parallel. For all models in the hierarchy, the configuration set parameter?CodeInterfacePackaging ?has the value?C++ class .   The error is as follows: CGIR assertion 'In getComponentInterfaceForModelRef: Assertion failed: mxProxyComponentInterface()' failed in 'B:\ matlab\ src\ rtwcg\ rtwcg_utils\ rtwcg_entryfcn_mgr. cpp:108'
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Warning message or dialog when saving MATLAB search path using savepath or Set Path GUI on Windows
Description: In MATLAB on Windows, the execution of the 'savepath' function or the use of the 'Set Path' GUI Tool to save a path may fail due to insufficient permissions to write the pathdef.m file.Users may encounter the following warning message when executing the 'savepath' function in MATLAB:?>> savepath?Warning: Unable to save path to file 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2023b\toolbox\local\pathdef.m'. You can save your path to a different location?by calling SAVEPATH with an input argument that specifies the full path. For MATLAB to use that path in future sessions, save the path to 'pathdef.m' in your MATLAB startup folder.   Or they may receive a dialog message from the 'Set Path' GUI Tool when clicking on the 'save' button: MATLAB cannot save changes to the path. The path file, pathdef.m, might be read-only or might be in a directory for which you do not have write access. You can save pathdef.m to a different location. In order for MATLAB to use that pathdef.m in future sessions, you need to save it in your MATLAB startup directory (the directory from which you start MATLAB). Would you like to save pathdef.m to another location??
Workaround: Run the following code once in?the?MATLAB Command Window:[status, cmdout] = system(['attrib -r "', fullfile(matlabroot, 'toolbox', 'local', 'pathdef.m'), '"'], 'runAsAdmin') If a User Account Control dialog appears, click on "Yes" to proceed.After running the code, you should see that the 'status' is '0' and 'cmdout' is an '0x0 empty array'. This indicates that the code has executed successfully. Then, you should be able to continue saving your path as usual.If the status returned by this command is not '0', you should eitherFollow the instructions in the warning message to save 'pathdef.m' to a different location,Or, ask the administrator of your installation to change the permissions of the file whose path is given by the following command:? ? ? ? fullfile(matlabroot, 'toolbox', 'local', 'pathdef.m')to make it writable.

Summary: Simulating a model directly after receiving the Insufficient memory budget for delay expressions history?error might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If a model involves delays, and insufficient memory budget has been allocated in the Solver Configuration block, simulating the model twice in a row might cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Do one of the following:?Close the model after receiving this error, and then reopen it before simulating again. Increase the Delay memory budget [kB] in the Solver Configuration block and then simulate again.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Simulating Model blocks in accelerator mode might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: Simulating Model blocks in accelerator mode might cause MATLAB to crash when all these conditions apply: The model reference hierarchy contains at least three levels. A Model block that simulates in normal mode is in a conditionally executed subsystem, such as an enabled subsystem. That Model block references a model that contains at least one Model block that simulates in accelerator mode  The generated stack trace contains lines similar to:? [? 0] 0x00007ffe3045f364 <matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_lang_exec_blocks.dll+00586596 SubsystemExecBlock::GetContextSubSystemRanBCTransitionDworkPtr+00000020[? 1] 0x00007ffe3256ace2?<matlabroot>\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+11250914 slNotifyStateflowLoaded+00118146[? 2] 0x00007ffe325787ff?<matlabroot>\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+11307007 slNotifyStateflowLoaded+00174239[? 3] 0x00007ffe3257608b?<matlabroot>\bin\win64\libmwsimulink.dll+11296907 slNotifyStateflowLoaded+00164139[? 4] 0x00007ffdfc7185ec <working_directory>\myModelRef_msf.mexw64+00034284 mexFunction+00030188[? 5] 0x00007ffe30c5931c?<matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+07639836 SetSFcnExportsFunctionCalls+00033676[? 6] 0x00007ffe30c6e8e5 <matlabroot>\bin\win64\sl_lang_blocks.dll+07727333 CallCMexSFcnSetupRuntimeResources+00000133?

Summary: The tdmsDatastore read function returns false data after 2^32 samples
Description: When attempting to read files of a tdmsDatastore with channels exceeding 2^32 samples, at that point the reading wraps around and repeats data from the beginning of the file instead. So all data returned after 2^32 samples is incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Data transfer protections might not be used when multiple tasks write to the global variable for root outport blocks using union sample time
Description: When a global variable associated with root outport blocks is written to by multiple tasks, data transfer protections might not be used and the data might be susceptible to data corruption when all of these conditions are true:? Config setting Allow multiple tasks to access inputs and outputs  ?is enabled. Root outport block is connected to a Merge block that has source blocks in different tasks. Root outport block is connected to a function-call subsystem or a Simulink function with callers in different tasks. Root outport block uses propagated union sample time.

Summary: Simulation mismatch between the MATLAB code and the generated HDL/HLS code
Description: If an operand has a non-zero fractional part in fixed-point arithmetic (addition/subtraction), it can lead to a numeric mismatch of up to 1 LSB (Least Significant Bit). Code generation for the following MATLAB function using the HDL Workflow Advisor with fixed-point conversion enabled results in a numerical mismatch. The mismatch occurs because the expressions 'x + pi/2' and 'x - pi/2' include a constant of a non-zero fractional length. function y = f(x)  ??y = x;  ??if?(x > pi/2)  ??????y = x - pi/2;  ??elseif (x < -pi/2)  ??????y = x + pi/2;  ??end  end?

Summary: IP core generation with AXI4-Stream interface may error out when AXI4-Stream port rate is not an integer multiple of the DUT base rate.
Description: During the IP core generation workflow when you use AXI4-Stream interface that has multiple rates, HDL Workflow Advisor may error out if the AXI4-Stream port rates are not an integer multiple of the DUT base rate. HDL code generation fails at the?3.2 ?Generate RTL Code and IP Core ?with a following error message: Replication factors must be a row vector of integers or integer scalars. ?

Summary: Model diagram is not editable in SimBiology Model Builder if a project contain multiple models
Description: If you open a SimBiology project with multiple models and try to open the diagram from the Project  tab in the Browser  panel for any model except the first one, the diagram opens but is unresponsive.
Workaround: First close the project and reopen it in the app. In the Browser panel, select Model. Then use the model selection drop-down menu to switch models and see the diagram.After a model has been selected from the drop-down menu at least once, its diagram becomes editable from the Project tab also.

Summary: DSP System Toolbox HDL Support / Sources/ Sine wave block throws an internal error during HDL code generation.
Description: DSP System Toolbox HDL Support / Sources/ Sine wave block??throws an internal error during HDL code generation when Samples per frame  are more than 1.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Combination of parfor loop with a reduction variable and a nested loop that writes to or reads from cell arrays
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect answers if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions:? Your MATLAB code contains a parfor  loop. Another loop is nested inside this parfor  loop. This inner loop reads from or writes to a cell array. This cell array is heterogeneous (that is, different cell array elements contain different data types) or is created using the cell()  function.? The parfor  loop contains a reduction variable (that is, a variable updated using the syntax var = var op someValue . ?For example, var = var + 1  or var = var * C . This reduction operation itself might or might not be inside the inner loop.  In such situations, the generated code might produce an incorrect result for the final computed value of the reduction variable. Here is an example MATLAB function that is affected by this issue: function out = xUnrollCellInParforx = 0;parfor j = 1:10 % parallel for loop    c = cell(1,2); % variable created using cell() function    for k = 1:2        c{k} = k;    end    x = x + 1; % x is a reduction variableendout = coder.const(x); % the value of x here is incorrectend

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code for a model that has TLC code that updates a ground value
Description: MATLAB might crash when generating code if a block has TLC code that attempts to update a built-in or alias data type ground value to an incorrect value.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Error while running MEX functions generated for MATLAB functions creating matrices of?less than intmax() elements
Description: You might get an error while running a MEX function generated from a MATLAB function which creates a matrix that meet these conditions:You create the matrix using a function whose arguments represent the size dimensions. For example, the function NaN . Any argument corresponding to the second size or later is a variable or a function of a variable. For example, NaN(x, y) , when using the?X = nan(sz1,...,szN ) form of syntax.? The argument is not guaranteed to be a positive integer.?  You might receive the following error even if the matrix being created by the MATLAB function does not have more than?intmax() ?elements : ? ? Cannot grow a matrix with greater than?intmax()?elements.
Workaround: To create the matrix with the MATLAB function, instead of using arguments that represent size dimensions, use a vector input whose elements represent the size dimension of the output.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2024a

Summary: From Spreadsheet block parameter Extrapolation before first data point has no effect when Range parameter includes range with only header row
Description: The Range  parameter of the From Spreadsheet block can include multiple, comma-separated ranges. If one of these ranges is only the first row (e.g. A1:C1,A5:C20 ), the block ignores the?Extrapolation before first data point? parameter. The data before the range is output according to the value of the parameter Data interpolation within time range .
Workaround: Modify the Range parameter to no longer include the header row. For example, if the Range was A1:C1, A5:C20, change the range to A5:C20.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 3, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using coder.gpu.persistentMemory inside recursive function
Description: The generated GPU code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code uses the?coder.gpu.persistentMemory  pragma inside a recursive function. For example, the code generated for the entryPoint  MATLAB function produces incorrect results. function x = entryPointx = foo(1);endfunction x = foo(n)coder.inline('never');persistent pif isempty(p)    p = 0;endcoder.gpu.persistentMemory(p);if coder.const(n == 1)    p = p + 1;    foo(0);endp = p + 1;x = p;end

Summary: Interprocess Data Read/Write blocks with channel 0 interrupt does not work in C2000 Microcontroller Blockset
Description: When a model with Interprocess Data Read/Write blocks is configured with hardware boards?TI Delfino F2837xD, TI Delfino F2837xD Launchpad or TI F2838x, and is configured to be triggered by CIPC0_INT or CMtoCPUXIPC0_INT in the Hardware Mapping tool, then the interrupt service routine triggered by IPC0 interrupt does not get executed.

Summary: Parameter tunability loss with System Mask
Description: Ability to tune the parameter in the generated code is lost for instance specific parameters on the model reference block which is referring a model with System Mask.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Simulink function called from Function Caller block in Initialize Function block or Terminate Function block might product incorrect output
Description: Output of a Simulink function called by a Function Caller block inside an Initialize Function block or a Terminate Function block might be incorrect under the following scenarios: Inside the Initialize Function block, the Function Caller block fails to invoke the Simulink function if this function is called by other Function Caller blocks, which are inside a Reset Function block, a Reinitialize Function block, or a Terminate Function block.  Inside the Terminate Function block, the Function Caller block fails to invoke a Simulink function when all of the following occurs.?  The function and the Terminate Function block containing its caller are not in the same model.?   All the other Function Caller blocks of this function are inside an Initialize Function block, a Reset Function block, or a Reinitialize Function block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Multiply-Add block may generate incorrect HDL code when Output data type is not Inherit via internal rule
Description: When the Output data type of Multiply-Add is not Inherit via internal rule, the generated HDL code may have different precision than expected for the addition. The code may also be inconsistent with HDL code generated by a Product block followed by a Sum block with same data type settings as the Multiply-Add block.

Summary: Training or making predictions with a?DAGNetwork?object errors for networks with Convolution2DLayer objects with non-symmetric padding
Description: When training or making predictions with a? DAGNetwork ?object, the software can throw an error for networks with?Convolution2DLayer ?objects with non-symmetric padding. Unrecognized function or variable 'iPadArray'.    Error in nnet.internal.cnn.layer.util.ConvolutionAddReLUGPUStrategy/forward (line 28)  ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? X = iPadArray(X, paddingSize);  The error can happen when all of these conditions are met: The model contains a Convolution2DLayer  object with non-symmetric padding (for example, when the Padding  option is [2 2 0 1] ?or in some cases when the Padding  option  "same" ). One of these: An AdditionLayer ?object then a ReLULayer ?object immediately follow the?Convolution2DLayer ?object in the network A BatchNormalizationLayer  object, an AdditionLayer ?object, then a ReLULayer ?object immediately follow the?Convolution2DLayer ?object in the network  The?ExecutionEnvironment  training or prediction option is  "gpu"  (or  "auto"  when a GPU is available to use).  If using the prediction function, the? Acceleration  option is  "auto" .
Workaround: As a workaround, try one of these options:Add a?FunctionLayer?object with function @(X)X?after the convolution layer.If training the network, train the network using a custom training loop.If making predictions with the network, convert the network to a dlnetwork object.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Undefined behavior in conversion from floating-point type to integer type
Description: The simulation and code generation of a Simulink model might be incorrect when all  of these are true: The Simulink model contains logic that converts a floating-point number to an?integer.  The integral part of the floating-point?number cannot be repre sented by the integer type.   For example, if your model contains a Saturation Dynamic block that has inputs of  double  type and output of  uint16  type, the following code might be generated. ????? tmp2 = (uint16_T)fmod(ceil(tmp1), 65536.0);  When? fmod(ceil(tmp1), 65536.0) < 0 , the conversion to  uint16_T  is an undefined behavior as per the C language standard, which means the result is unpredictable in this case.

Summary: Quick Start might change solver without notification
Description: The Simulink Coder Quick Start tool displays a list of configuration parameters that the Quick Start will apply to the model before code generation. Quick Start might change the setting of the Solver ?parameter without including the parameter in this list.

Summary: Incorrect value for statement coverage in the expected results for MATLAB Test validation test MLT_CodeCov_005
Description: When executing the IEC Certification Kit validation test suite for MATLAB Test, the expected results in these code coverage reports provides an incorrect value for statement coverage:    expected_results/CodeCoverage/index.html  expected_results/CodeCoverage.docx  expected_results/CodeCoverage.pdf?   In  MLT_CodeCov_005/testForLoopDecisionCoverage  at line 127, the eight element is incorrect.   Incorrect value:  0.333333333333333  Correct value is 0.666666666666667    This incorrect value hides a known issue in MATLAB that marks a for-loop condition as not covered when the condition is satisfied on the first instance. For more information, see ?Statement coverage is incorrect for a for-loop whose body does not run .?

Summary: Unable to get verification status for links from test sequence steps in external test harnesses?
Description: When you create links from a test step inside a test sequence block that is inside an external test harness, Requirements Toolbox  cannot get the correct value of the verification status for the requirement.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 6, R2024a

Summary: Extend using existing test data?parameter may incorrectly report objective status.
Description: Extend using existing test data ?parameter may incorrectly report objective status as Satisfied by existing tests . The input test case may fail to satisfy objective condition during simulation.?
Workaround: For such objectives, select?Simulate tests and generate coverage report?and inspect the coverage report to confirm the objective status.

Summary: The MaxStep?model parameter cannot be tuned when the RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck option is set to off?in RapidAccelerator mode simulations
Description: When the RapidAcceleratorUpToDateCheck ?option is set to off , the MaxStep ?model parameter becomes unchangeable. Instead, the MaxStep  value from the build time is used during the simulation, without any notification.?

Summary: Initial condition parameters in some Aerospace Blockset blocks not tunable in generated code
Description: The following blocks have tunable initial condition parameters that do not actually become tunable in the generated code when the Default parameter behavior  option is set to Inlined  on the Code Generation > Optimization  pane. Spacecraft Dynamics Orbit Propagator Attitude Dynamics 3DOF (Body Axes) and 3DOF (Wind Axes) 4th Order Point Mass (Longitudinal),?4th Order Point Mass Forces (Longitudinal),?6th Order Point Mass (Coordinated Flight),?6th Order Point Mass Forces (Coordinated Flight), and?Fixed-Wing Point Mass

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Schedule events from C action language charts are not honored
Description: When you trigger a schedule event (events that are sent to the Schedule Editor to schedule the execution of aperiodic partitions) from a Stateflow chart that uses C as the action language nothing happens.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 1, R2024a

Summary: When a ROS 2 node is generated using?rosReadImage with the optional "Encoding" argument, an error is thrown about an unrecognized parameter
Description: When generating a ROS 2 node from a MATLAB script using the syntax below:   imgData = rosReadImage(msg,"Encoding","rgb8"); The following error is thrown:   'rgb8' is not a recognized parameter.   For a list of valid name-value pair arguments, see the documentation for this function.
Workaround: Replace the syntax with:  imgData = rosReadImage(msg,0,"Encoding","rgb8");Note that this workaround is specifically for the code generation workflow. For MATLAB interpretation, there is no need to specify additional input arguments or encoding types.

Summary: Sample time colors might obscure corner colors for Model blocks
Description: When you view sample time colors in a Simulink model that contains a Model block, the sample time color might make it difficult to see the corners of the Model block. This might make it difficult to determine if the Model block is running in normal or accelerator mode.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using a For Each Subsystem block in AUTOSAR might generate incorrect code when allowed stack size is limited
Description: Incorrect code might be generated for a model when all?of the following conditions are met:The model is an AUTOSAR model. The model contains a For Each Subsystem block. Signals that are connected to the For Each Subsystem introduce internal buffers. An internal buffer is larger than the Maximum stack size (bytes) ?model configuration parameter or the TLC variable?MaxStackVariableSize .  Consider this incorrect generated code.void model_Step(void){? ? sint32 ForEach_itr;? ? // ...? ? /* Inport: '<Root>/In1' */? ? (void)Rte_Read_ERIWD_ERIWD? ? (model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].tmpRead);?? ? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 500; ForEach_itr++) {? ? ? ? // ...? ? ? ? /* Update for UnitDelay: '<S1>/Unit Delay' incorporates:? ? ? ? ?* ForEachSliceSelector generated from: '<S1>/InSS'? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? ? model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].UnitDelay_DSTATE =? ? ? ? ? ?model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].tmpRead[ForEach_itr];? ? }} The code generates a temporary buffer,?tmpRead , for the ExplicitReceive?in each of the iterations, instead of only once. See the following correct generated code.void model_Step(void){? ? sint32 ForEach_itr;? ? // ...? ? /* Inport: '<Root>/In1' */? ? (void)Rte_Read_ERIWD_ERIWD? ? (model_ARID_DEF.tmpRead); ?? ? for (ForEach_itr = 0; ForEach_itr < 500; ForEach_itr++) {? ? ? ? // ...? ? ? ? /* Update for UnitDelay: '<S1>/Unit Delay' incorporates:? ? ? ? ?* ForEachSliceSelector generated from: '<S1>/InSS'? ? ? ? ?*/? ? ? ? model_ARID_DEF.ARID_DEF_CoreSubsys[ForEach_itr].UnitDelay_DSTATE =? ? ? ? ? ?model_ARID_DEF.tmpRead[ForEach_itr] ;? ? }}
Workaround: If possible, increase both the MaxStackSize?and the TLC variable?MaxStackVariableSize?to be larger than the internal buffer.If stack size is a hard requirement (for example, due to target constraints), applying some or all of the following might work around this issue.?Disable the optimization option?LocalBlockOutputs.Insert a Signal Conversion block between the For Each Subsystem block and the block that generates the internal buffer in question. Enable the Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization?parameter for the Signal Conversion block.If the internal buffer is generated for root I/O due to a specific DataAccessMode (for example, ExplicitReceive), try to change the DataAccessMode.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 7, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: HDL code generation fails for the models that have blocks from DSP Builder Advanced (DSPBA) Blockset.
Description: When a model contains blocks from the DSP Builder Advanced Blockset, HDL code generation throws the following error message:? ?Unrecognized method, property, or field 'modelName' for class 'slhdlcoder.HDLCoder'.

Summary: Serial Link Designer and Parallel Link Designer apps error while opening projects on some computers not localized to English
Description: Computers localized to some regions including the continental Europe (Germany,France, Italy, and the Scandinavian countries) use some characters other thanthe comma (',') for the thousandth separator and the period ('.') for thedecimal separator. On these computers, Parallel Link Designer and SerialLink?Designer apps generate many error messages while opening any projectand the project opens incorrectly.
Workaround: On Windows machines, it is possible to load a language pack for English localized to the country where the problem occurs. For example, you can load a language pack for English (Germany). Then you can set the Regional Format to this (English (Germany) in this case) language pack while leaving the region settings at the desired country.??Linux??installations require localizing the entire machine to a language that uses the comma and the period as in English. To localize a Linux machine to English US: In your terminal, enter     the following command:$ localeThis will provide with all the locale settingscurrently in effect. To make sure that     numeric types are according to ˇ°en_US.UTF-8ˇ±, execute the following     commands in the terminal:     $ sudo update-locale LC_CTYPE=en_US.UTF-8? ? ? ? ? ?$ sudo update-locale LC_NUMERIC=en_US.UTF-8 Restart your machine.     Upon restart, you can confirm that the new locale settings are in effect     by going through step 1.

Summary: False positive violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A12-1-1 when using a delegating constructor
Description: Polyspace raises an incorrect violation of AUTOSAR-C++14 A12-1-1 when a constructor of a class without a data member uses a delegating constructor. // Interfaceclass IBar {}; // A class without data member.class Bar: public IBar {? Bar();? Bar(int i);};Bar::Bar(int i): IBar() {}Bar::Bar(): Bar(0) { // Incorrect violation}

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: svd?function with?certain input sizes and using cuSOLVER library
Description: GPU Coder might generate incorrect code for the?svd ?function if all  these? conditions are true: You generate?standalone code (static library, dynamically linked library, or executable program).  Input matrix to svd  has size m -by-n , where?m < n . You enable the use of the NVIDIA cuSOLVER library.  The issue occurs if the?svd ?function call is from a user-written function or? other MATLAB toolbox functions. To verify the existence of this issue in an application, enable GPU error checks in the generated code. For example, use the?SafeBuild  property of the GPU code configuration object. If the issue exists, the application might exit with a?CUSOLVER_STATUS_INVALID_VALUE ?error code .
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: Generated model might contain unconnected Compare to Constant blocks
Description: Instead of a Constant block, the generated model might contain the original Compare to Constant block without any connected inputs. This might occur In cases where the result of a Compare to Constant block is known at compile time, and HDL Coder optimizes?the Compare to Constant block logic, including input pipeline requests, by replacing the block with a Constant block.? However, the generated HDL code correctly reflects the operation as a constant.
Workaround: Requesting optimizations like Distributed Pipelining will force the model generation process to render the optimized Compare to Constant operator as the correct constant operation.

Summary: Custom?DefinitionFile?attribute is ignored for Simulink.VariantVariable with Simulink.Parameter as choice values.
Description: Custom?DefinitionFile?attribute is ignored for?Simulink.VariantVariable ?with?Simulink.Parameter ?as choice values in the below scenario:? Consider a Simulink.VariantVariable  kv  with Simulink.Parameter  objects kp1  and kp2  as choice values: ?% Create a variant control V = Simulink.VariantControl('Value', 1, 'ActivationTime', 'startup');  % Create choice parameterskp1 = Simulink.Parameter(4.5);kp1?.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'Custom';kp1?.CoderInfo.CustomStorageClass = 'Const';kp1?.CoderInfo.CustomAttributes.HeaderFile = 'param.h';kp1?.CoderInfo.CustomAttributes.DefinitionFile = 'param.c';  kp2 = Simulink.Parameter(8.5);kp2?.CoderInfo.StorageClass = 'Custom';kp2?.CoderInfo.CustomStorageClass = 'Const';kp2?.CoderInfo.CustomAttributes.HeaderFile = 'param.h';kp2?.CoderInfo.CustomAttributes.DefinitionFile = 'param.c';  % Create variant parameterkv = Simulink.VariantVariable('Choices', {'V == 1', 'kp1', 'V == 2', 'kp2'});?  In the generated code, kp1  and kp2  are defined in model.c  instead of param.c .?

Summary: Inter-processor communication blocks (Interprocess Data Write and Read) do not work as expected with higher level compiler optimization
Description: When compiler optimization level is -O2 or higher, reveals issue with IPC driver code. The CPUx halts in IPC driver code when it is waiting for an update from CPUy through the shared variable. Compiler ignores volatile keyword causing the issue.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when loading architecture model with referenced architecture subsystem
Description: When loading a System Composer model with a Reference Component block that references another architecture subsystem, MATLAB may crash.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when modifying a table in a parfor loop
Description: MATLAB might crash on Windows machines when modifying a table in a parfor  loop. This crash can occur when you modify table variables in a parfor  loop run from the Command Window. For example, in this code MATLAB crashes when you modify the table variable named Var1 . parfor i=1:5    temptab = table(["a";"b"],VariableNames="Var1");    temptab.Var1 = ["c";"d"];end

Summary: Mask parameter value changes from 'true' to 'on' on editing its mask definition from Mask Editor
Description: While editing and saving the mask definition from the Mask Editor, if an edit mask parameter's value is set to 'true', it gets changed to 'on'.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Wrong port of Multiport Switch block might be selected in code generation execution on Apple silicon
Description: Normal mode simulation and code generation result mismatch might occur under these conditions: Your machine has an Apple silicon CPU The model contains Multiport Switch block with a signal with a floating point data type connected to the control port That signal value becomes?NaN ?at some point   During code generation, based on the behavior of an out-of-range floating point-to-integer cast of the control port input, a different data port might be selected. This cast is undefined in C and the behavior is platform-specific. During normal mode simulation, this behavior does not occur.?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when modifying a table from the Debug Command Window
Description: MATLAB might crash on Windows machines when modifying a table from the Debug Command Window. This crash can occur when you modify table variables after changing the workspace within the debugger. For example: after hitting a breakpoint and using the dbup  function, executing t.Var1(1) = 1;  to modify an existing variable Var1  of a table named t  might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: From Spreadsheet block errors when specified range does not contain any empty cells
Description: The From Spreadsheet block displays this error even when a spreadsheet range does not contain any empty cells: The sheet contains either empty signal headings or leading/trailing blank cells. For the header row, either provide values for each column or clear the values for all the columns.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: HDL Coder may generate incorrect code for a multi-rate subsystem when latency is introduced upstream and clock-rate pipelining is enabled
Description: The generated HDL code may be incorrect when all  these conditions are met: There is downsampling from a rate faster than the clock-rate to a slower rate. Clock-rate pipelining is enabled in the region where the downsampling occurs. Additional latency (not at clock-rate) is introduced at a higher level than the downsampling in the subsystem hierarchy and upstream from it.
Workaround: To verify whether the generated HDL code is correct:Generate a validation model.Check if there is any numerical mismatch.

Summary: Specifying a timetable or timeseries in Satellite Scenario pointAt method results in an error if the step size of the data is less than 1e-12 seconds
Description: Specifying a timetable  or timeseries ?in the pointAt  method of Satellite ?and?Gimbal ?objects will result in an error if the step size of the data is less than 1e-12 seconds (1 picosecond).
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 7, R2024a

Summary: Cannot set excitation voltage?for bridge measurement on some devices
Description: When you try to set bridge measurement excitation voltage to 3.3 v,? the setting reverts to its last value with no indication of error. This was observed on an NI-9237, and can be reproduced using a simulated device. Related devices might also be affected.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Delay balancing can result in an error for a feedback loop through hierarchy even though clock-rate pipelining is enabled
Description: When a Simulink model has a feedback loop going through subsystem hierarchy, clock-rate pipelining might not reliably handle the full loop in the presence of minimal optimization requests (i.e., pipeline requests only exist at the top level of loop hierarchy while the state for the loop exists at a lower level). This might lead to unexpected delay balancing errors for feedback loops.
Workaround: Enabling?FlattenHierarchy?on the top level of the feedback loop hierarchy can help to resolve a delay balancing error.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Dataflow conversion error during HDL code generation of for-loops with over 100 iterations
Description: HDL Coder may throw an error when generating HDL code from MATLAB code for the following conditions: The code is being generated from a MATLAB Function block with the HDL block parameter Architecture  set to MATLAB Datapath  The code is being generated from a MATLAB source file with the option Aggressive Dataflow Conversion ?enabled in the MATLAB HDL Workflow Advisor.  In either case, generating code from a for-loop with more than 100 iterations, or a nested for-loop with more than 100 total iterations, might not work. For example, the following constructs may fail:for i=1:100    % codeend orfor j=1:10    for k=1:10        % code    endend The error is similar to: Failed Error:Dataflow conversion error: Function2SubsystemConverter.evalExpression: 0: variable description not found for the specified loop iteration Error using hdlcoder.pirctx/earlyElaborate Error:Dataflow conversion error: Function2SubsystemConverter.evalExpression: 0: variable description not found for the specified loop iteration

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Unnecessary casts in the generated code for a model with symbolic dimensions
Description: The code generator might produce unnecessary casts in the generated code for a model that meets all of these conditions: The model contains a signal,?rtu_u , ?which is a matrix with symbolic dimensions such as A*B  or A+B . A Bus Assignment block assigns rtu_u ?to a nonsymbolic element of a bus,rtu_y . A Selectorblock selects an index of rtu_u ?which an Assignment block then assigns to anindex of the nonsymbolic bus element rtu_y .??  For example, suppose rtu_u  has dimensions AxB  withvalues of 2 and 3 and rtu_y  has nonsymbolic dimensions of 2x3 .? The unnecessary cast is generated for rtu_u  when assigned to rtu_y . The incorrect generated code will show?rtu_y[0] = (*(real_T (*)[6])&rtu_u)[0]  instead of correctly showing?rtu_y[0] = rtu_u[0] .
Workaround: If symbolic dimension propagation is not required for the code generation, clear the?Allow symbolic dimension specification configuration parameter.If the symbolic dimensions are required in the generated code, then you should change the dimension of nonsymbolic signals to which the symbolic signal is assigned to its symbolic type in the model. For example, if the non-symbolic signal rtu_y with dimensions [2,3] is assigned a symbolic signal rtu_u with symbolic dimension [A,B], change the dimension of rtu_y from [2,3] to [A,B] and assign the value of A with 2 and B with 3 from base workspace to both maximum and minimum fields.
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 2, R2024a

Summary: MATLAB might crash when cutting elements of an architecture model
Description: A selection cut operation (e.g. Ctrl-X on Windows) involving System Composer models might cause MATLAB to crash when edit-time checking is turned on.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Selector block configured with the complex inputs might generate incorrect HDL Code.
Description: HDL Coder generates incorrect HDL Code, when your model has a Selector block that is configured with complex inputs and Index Option ?is set to Index vector (port) , Starting Index (port) ?or Starting and ending indices (port) . The generated HDL Code might fail to compile in synthesis tools.?
Workaround: Consider using Complex to Real-Imag block at input of Selector block and Real-Imag to Complex block at output of Selector block.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Object or cell array that is field of a struct might have uninitialized data if the struct is an output of a multi-output function
Description: This issue occurs if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions: Your MATLAB code accesses data contained in an object or a cell array that is a field of a struct This struct is one of the outputs of a multi-output function call In the definition of the called function, one of the cell array elements (or object properties) is assigned in a code path, and is left unassigned in another code path.  In the generated code, the cell array (or the object) that the returned struct contains might have an uninitialized element (or property). Accessing this uninitialized data results in reading from uninitialized memory, thereby producing results that are incorrect and nondeterministic.? For example, consider this MATLAB code: function out = main(n)[out1, out2] = f(n);out = out1.cls{2} + out2;endfunction [out1, out2] = f(n)out1 = struct();out1.cls = cell(1,4);out2 = n;if n > 0    out1.cls{2} = 422;endend In MATLAB execution, calling main  with a non-positive input argument produces the empty array [] . In the generated code, such a call produces a nondeterministic value. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Make sure that all the object properties or cell array elements are assigned before returning them as outputs of the multi-output function call.

Summary: Dilation and Grayscale Dilation blocks return incorrect control signals when using a kernel width of 10 pixels with pixel input of 8 pixels per clock cycle
Description: Both the Dilation and Grayscale Dilation blocks will not generate hStart signals for lines after the first line?when using a kernel width of 10 pixels with pixel input of 8 pixels per clock cycle.

Summary: Simulation results might be incorrect for model reference hierarchies when top model or parent model disables zero-crossing detection
Description: When the top model or a parent model in a model reference hierarchy sets the Zero-crossing control ?parameter to  Disable All , simulation results might be incorrect if one or more referenced models in the hierarchy use a different setting for the Zero-crossing control ?parameter.   For incorrect simulation results to occur, all of these conditions must apply:? The top model must use a variable-step solver. The model hierarchy must contain continuous states. The referenced model that uses a different setting for the Zero-crossing control ?parameter must contain blocks that support zero-crossing detection, and the output of those blocks must affect continuous state values.  This bug affects results in simulation only and does not affect code generated from the model or results computed using generated code.
Workaround: To avoid this issue, choose one of these options:Change the value of the Zero-crossing control?parameter in the top model and parent models to a value other than Disable All.Set the Zero-crossing control?parameter to Disable All?for all models in the model reference hierarchy.

Summary: Serial packets not received correctly when terminator character is present in actual data
Description: If you use the Serial Receive block with a terminator and your data contains a terminator value, then that value is interpreted as an actual terminator and can cause an error during simulation.
Workaround: If your header is "H" and terminator is "T", you can change the header field in block to ''TH" and leave the terminator parameter empty. For this workaround, even though you miss the first packet, you receive the remaining packets correctly.

Summary: HDL Coder may give warning when attempting to rename the locked library
Description: When you attempt to save the locked library system with a new name, the following warning message appears in the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer  indicating an attempt to modify the locked library: Warning:Error executing PostNameChange callback "HDLCCModelRenameCallback" for block diagram "myLibr2": Attempt to modify 'myLibr2' which is a locked (read-only) library ?? This warning does not have impact on saving the library with the new name.?
Workaround: To avoid this warning, unlock the library and then save it with a desired name.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size mismatch of empty fixed-point arrays during MEX execution or extrinsic function call
Description: Executing the generated MEX might produce incorrect results or cause a MATLAB crash if the dimensions of empty fixed-point arrays passed between MATLAB and MEX executions differ between code generation time and run time. This issue might occur in two distinct situations: Input size mismatch during MEX execution:  During code generation : You generate a MEX function that accepts an empty fixed-point array. During MEX execution : You pass an empty fixed-point array to the generated MEX function. The first dimension of this array does not match the size you specified during code generation.  For example, consider this MATLAB code: function y = foo(x)y = x;end Generate a MEX file for this function at the command line, using a variable size array of fixed-point values as the input type: t1 = coder.typeof(fi(0), [1, 10], [0, 1]);codegen foo -args t1 Define t2   as an empty array of fixed-point values, the first dimension of which differs in size to t1 : t2 = fi(zeros(0,3)) In MATLAB execution, calls to  foo(t2)  return an empty 0x3  array, while calls to?foo_mex(t2) ?return a 1x3  array of undefined values. Output size mismatch in extrinsic function call:  You call an extrinsic function that returns an empty array of fixed-point values You assign the output of the extrinsic function to a preinitialized variable The first dimension of this output is a different size to the first dimension of the preinitialized variable  For example, consider these MATLAB functions: function y = my_extrinsic()y = fi(zeros(0, 3));endfunction y = foo2()y = repmat(fi(0), [1 5]);coder.varsize('y', [1 5], [0 1])coder.extrinsic('my_extrinsic');y = my_extrinsic();end In MATLAB execution, calls to foo2  return an empty 0x3  array, while calls to  foo2_mex ?return a  1x3  array of undefined values.

Summary: MATLAB might crash on startup on Mac
Description: MATLAB on macOS might crash on startup. The generated stack trace contains lines similar to:? [? 8] 0x00000001cd88adb6 /Applications/MATLAB_R2023a.app/bin/maci64/Frameworks/Chromium Embedded Framework.framework/Chromium Embedded Framework+58797494 ChromeAppModeStart_v6+08658918[? 9] 0x00000001cd88b011 /Applications/MATLAB_R2023a.app/bin/maci64/Frameworks/Chromium Embedded Framework.framework/Chromium Embedded Framework+58798097 ChromeAppModeStart_v6+08659521[ 10] 0x00000001ca0aef11 /Applications/MATLAB_R2023a.app/bin/maci64/Frameworks/Chromium Embedded Framework.framework/Chromium Embedded Framework+00225041 cef_request_context_create_context+00000273[ 11] 0x00000001befda4a0 /Applications/MATLAB_R2023a.app/bin/maci64/libjcef.dylib+00804000 _ZN10nativeJcef30addPlatformSpecificCommandLineERK13CefStringBaseI20CefStringTraitsUTF16E13scoped_refptrI14CefCommandLineEbb+00336064?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Object returned as output from a function call might have uninitializedproperty values
Description: The generated code might set one or more properties of an object incorrectly if your MATLAB code satisfies?all ?these conditions:  You define multiple instances of a class, at least one of which (say, obj ) has an undefined property. Your MATLAB code passes two or more objects?to a function foo . One of these input objects is (or contains)?obj .  The function  foo  returns an output that depends on at least two of its input objects, one of which is (or contains) obj . For some execution paths, the output of foo  is the input object that is (or contains) obj .  In such situations, the code generator does not recognize that some properties of the returned class are undefined and instead sets the undefined properties to wrong or uninitialized values. For example, consider this MATLAB code: classdef MyClass    properties        p    endendfunction out = main(n)    c1 = MyClass;    c2 = MyClass;    c2.p = 42;    r = foo(c1, c2, n);    out = r.p;endfunction out = foo(c1, c2, n)    out = c2;    if n        out = c1;    endend In MATLAB execution, calls to main   with argument true   return an empty array ([] ). If you generate a MEX file for main , calls to main_mex  with argument true  return an undefined value. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.?
Workaround: If your code includes a function that returns an object depending on multiple object arguments, make sure that all properties of all passed objects are initialized before the function call.

Summary: HDL Model Advisor check for nonzero output latency may pass when the delay block adjacent to block having latency is comment through.
Description: When your model contains a block having nonzero latency and you add the adjacent delays to match latency. The HDL Code Advisor Check for blocks that havenonzero output latency  might pass even when the adjacent delay blocks are comment through.?

Summary: Incorrect number of output ports for SIL block generated from subsystem
Description: A SIL block generated from a subsystem might not have the correct number of output ports when all these conditions apply: The subsystem contains a Model block. That Model block has a port that outputs a nonvirtual bus. That port corresponds with an Outport block in the referenced model.
Workaround: For each Outport block that provides a nonvirtual bus to one or more blocks in the parent subsystem, select Output as nonvirtual bus in parent model.

Summary: Generated HDL code of the For Each Subsystem may fail to compile in the synthesis tool when it is configured with the mask parameters.??
Description: The generated HDL code of the For Each Subsystem might be incorrect and may fail to compile in the synthesis tool if the following conditions are true:? When the For Each Subsystem is configured with the mask parameters.? When the blocks under the For Each Subsystem at any hierarchy are configured to use generic parameters.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect simulation results for Just-In-Time Accelerator mode when comparing overflow unsigned integers on Apple silicon systems
Description: Incorrect simulation results in Just-In-Time Accelerator mode for a model that meets all  of these conditions: The model contains a MinMax block that takes two inputs of unsigned integer type. The inputs are smaller than 32  bits. An input to the MinMax block is derived from a computation where an integer overflow occurs without saturation.  When simulating a model that meets the above conditions in Just-In-Time Accelerator mode, it is?possible to obtain incorrect outputs from the MinMax block, specifically for Apple silicon systems.
Workaround: For the input signal of the MinMax block which is derived from the computation where an integer overflow occurs, use a Data Type Conversion block and enable the conversion of the signal to a 32-bit equivalent integer value. Then, specify the output signal data type of the MinMax block to the original desired integer type.

Summary: Generating code for an AUTOSAR model results in MATLAB crash due to presence of a Message Merge block
Description: Generating code for an AUTOSAR model results in a MATLAB crash if following conditions are met. The model logic creates messages, either by using Message Send blocks or using Stateflow message send ?operator. The model contains two or more of these output message lines. Message lines are merged using a Message Merge block.  The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines:?? slmsg::MsgCGReservoir::constructMsgFcn slmsg::MsgCGReservoir::getValidMsgFcn?  The Message Merge block is only supported for AUTOSAR?models to simulate composition-level communication between model references. Message Merge blocks should not be used in a component model to generate code.
Workaround: Remove the Message Merge block from the AUTOSAR component model. Message Merge blocks can be used in the model hierarchy for simulation?to model RTE behavior of N:1 communication.

Summary: Mismatch in simulation and generated code results for Data Conversion operation with floor rounding mode
Description: When using the data conversion block for (LREAL/REAL to UINT) with the rounding mode set to the floor, there is a mismatch in the simulation and generated code results for negative number inputs. This occurs when all the following are true:(LREAL/REAL to UINT) type data conversion. Floor rounding mode selected. Negative numbers as input.  In other data type conversions, such as (LREAL/REAL to INT), a custom rt_floor function is missing in this case.?

Summary: Interprocess Data Read/Write blocks does not work with bus input types for mixed data types in C2000 Microcontroller Blockset
Description: When a model is configured with hardware boards such as TI Delfino F2837xD, TI Delfino F2837xD Launchpad or TI F2838x, and is configured to send mixed data types in a bus using the Interprocess Data Read/Write blocks, then the data obtained on the receiving core does not match with the transmitted data.

Summary: Code generated for ARM Compute Library version 19.05 might produce segmentation fault if sequence data are passed to the?softmax layer
Description: You might encounter a segmentation fault when running the generated code?if?all ?of these conditions are true: The target library is set to ARM Compute Library version 19.05. The deep learning network has a softmax layer. Sequence data is passed to the?softmax layer as input.
Workaround: Use ARM Compute Library version 20.02.1 or later.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Using uninitialized property of a value object returned from a function might not produce an error
Description: The generated code might use an uninitialized property of a value object, thereby producing incorrect results, if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions:? A function  foo  is passed an input argument that is (or contains) a handle object  h  foo ?returns a value object?v ?that has uninitialized properties v ?is a property of the handle object?h   For example, consider this MATLAB entry-point function bar . ?Here,?HC  is a handle class with a property hp  and VC  is a value class with a property vp . function out = barh = HC;h.hp = VC;v = foo(h);out = v.vp;end Before the function?foo ?is called , the property vp  of the instance of VC ?that is assigned to h.hp  is uninitialized. The function foo  is defined as: function outV = foo(inH)outV = inH.hp;inH.hp.vp = 1;end foo ?returns a copy of the instance of VC ?for which the property?vp  is uninitialized. The property?vp ?of this returned copy?is then read by the function bar . In the generated code for the the entry-point function bar , reading the property vp  of this returned copy results in reading uninitialized memory. This result also does not match the result in MATLAB execution, which is an empty array. This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.
Workaround: Make sure that the properties of the copy of the value object in the called function are initialized before the function returns the value object.For example, change the above definition of foo to:function outV = foo(inH)inH.hp.vp = 1;outV = inH.hp;end

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Subsequent builds generate incorrect code for AUTOSAR reference models that use lookup tables defined in base workspace
Description: Regenerating code for AUTOSAR models containing model references results in incorrect code if the following conditions are met.The top model contains a referenced model. The referenced model uses lookup tables. At least one lookup table is defined in the base workspace. Code is generated from the top model. Code is regenerated in the same MATLAB session without closing the model.  In this scenario, RTE function calls are used in the generated code for the model reference to access the lookup table data. The expected code should access global variables associated with the lookup tables instead.

Summary: FMU import to Simulink errors due to newline character sequence in modelDescription.xml file
Description: Importing an FMU into Simulink results in the following error message due the presence of? "&#xA" character sequence in the modelDescription.xml file: ?Character vector is not terminated properly . This occurs while exporting a model with bus object to a standalone FMU in Simulink. The newline character in the bus object description is converted to the "&#xA" within the modelDescription.xml file of the FMU.??
