Workaround: You can resolve this error using either one of the following methods:Manually remove "&#xA" from the modelDescription.xml fileSet FMU Import block parameter FMUCreateBusObject?to off

Summary: Compiled applications unable to log in to MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster running at Security Level 3
Description: When connecting to a MATLAB Job Scheduler cluster running at Security Level 3, a compiled MATLAB application hangs when prompting the user for a password. In the MATLAB Command Window, a message like the following appears: *** Authenticate Parallel Computing User ***  Enter <username>'s?password.:  The user is unable to enter and submit their password to the cluster.

Summary: In App Designer, axes sometimes appear distorted in Design View
Description: In App Designer, when axes are parented to a figure or a tab that has been resized, the axes render incorrectly in the canvas, appearing either stretched or squeezed. However, axes are displayed correctly in the running app.

Summary: Generating documents using Word templates that contain invalid XML causes MATLAB to crash
Description: Generating a document or document part using a Word template that has invalid XML, for example, a template that was manually modified outside of Word, might cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Do not edit Word XML directly. Instead, create and edit Word templates using Word or the DOM API mlreportgen.dom.Template class.For existing templates, some XML issues might be fixed by opening and saving the template in Word.

Summary: MATLAB might crash when using a grid layout with a custom background color
Description: MATLAB might crash when querying the BackgroundColor  property of a GridLayout  object created using the uigridlayout  function. The stack trace generated by MATLAB includes these lines: [  0] 0x00007ffd1c107a30 graphics::datatype::ColorBase::operator= at B:\matlab\src\hgdatatypes\src\ColorBase.cpp:211 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2023a\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\hgdatatypes.dll)[  1] 0x00007ffd1c0b4aa5 graphics::datatype::RGBAColor::operator= at B:\matlab\src\hgdatatypes\objects\RGBAColorDataTypeImpl.cpp:376 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2023a\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\hgdatatypes.dll)[  2] 0x00007ffd1c6be5b8 ui::container::FIFOContainer::fillInUpdateViewInfo at B:\matlab\src\hg\hg\gbtobjects\uicomponent\FIFOContainerImpl.cpp:193 (in \\mathworks\devel\bat\archive\R2023a\perfect\matlab\bin\win64\hg.dll)

Summary: Code generation might fail on Windows if hardware is set to ROS or ROS 2
Description: During code generation, if you set the hardware board to ROS or ROS 2 in the configuration settings, code generation might fail with an error like this: ??? The library method 'vision.internal.buildable.kdtreeBuildable.updateBuildInfo' failed.Caused by:    Unable to determine MEX compiler: use mex -setup to configure your system.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: MATLAB System block simulation output might be corrupt for messages with bus payloads
Description: The simulation results of the MATLAB System block output might be corrupt when all of the following conditions are true: The block's 'Simulate using ' parameter is configured to?Code generation ? The block's output is a message The message payload is a bus The bus contains fields with data types that are not double

Summary: Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor might cause error if there are commented PS-Simulink Converter blocks in Simscape model
Description: The Simscape HDL Workflow Advisor issues the error 'Array indices must be positive integers or logical values' while generating the HDL implementation model if the original Simscape model contains PS-Simulink Converter blocks that have been commented out for simulation.
Workaround: Remove all the commented PS-Simulink Converter blocks and the output ports associated with each commented PS-Simulink Converter block from the Simscape model.

Summary: Very slow importing of Simulink Test results containing temporal assessments
Description: It takes a very long time to import?Simulink Test results that contain many temporal assessments or temporal assessments for long-running simulations.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 4, R2024a

Summary: Traceability mapping issues between code for subsystem reference and corresponding model elements
Description: When you generate code for a Subsystem Reference block by?right-clicking the block and selecting C/C++ code  > Buildthis subsystem , there is an inconsistent mapping between the code identifiers and the model elements meaning that clicking certain code snippets does not highlight the corresponding model element and vice versa.  In some cases, you might also get an error in the form of 'Invalid SID: '<modelName>:<number>'.  No object corresponds to '<modelName>:<number>'.

Summary: Using cameras that have non-ASCII character property values might cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB might crash when creating a videoinput  object with a gige  or gentl  adaptor to connect to a camera that has property values containing non-ASCII characters.?The Goldeye G-034 TEC1 is one such camera. Non-ASCII characters include symbols, accented letters, non-Latin characters, and special characters.
Workaround: To work around this issue, try editing the relevant camera properties from the vendor software to remove any non-ASCII characters from their values.

Summary: Meshing errors with certain STL files that have bad features
Description: A check for detecting closed surface meshes results in errors at the meshing stage if the STL file contained bad features.

Summary: Variant Subsystems with Model Reference as the active choice, opens the referenced model in a new window on double click
Description: If the Simulink model contains a Variant Subsystem that has the Model Reference as the active choice, then double clicking on the Variant Subsystem will open up the referenced model in a new window. The expectation here is that the Model Reference will open in the same window as the Variant Subsystem.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Size of variable-size empty cell array might not correctly propagate via assignment
Description: The size of an empty cell array x  in the generated code might be incorrect if your MATLAB code satisfies both  of these conditions: You define a variable-size empty cell array?y  You set x  equal to y   For example, consider the following MATLAB function:? function x = foo()coder.varsize('y', [0 5], [0 1]);y = cell(0,3);x = y;end In MATLAB execution, calls to foo ?return an empty cell array of size  0x3 , while calls to  foo_mex  return an empty cell array of size  0x0 . ?  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Initialize Function block might not initialize some model states and result in incorrect simulation results
Description: Simulink might incorrectly skip states whose values are controlled by a Function Caller block inside an Initialize Function block when all of the following occur: A global Simulink function defined at the top-level model is called inside the Initialize Function block. The Initialize Function block is inside a referenced model. The Model block contains one or more output ports which propagate constant sample time.

Summary: When using frame-based HDL cosimulation, and the HDL port width is greater than 128 bits, the dimension ordering is non-standard.
Description: The HDL Cosimulation block interprets a [3x2] frame signal as a frame of 2 samples, each comprised of 3 Simulink elements.  The conventional?representation of frame-based signals in MathWorks products interprets a [3x2] frame signal as a frame of 3 samples, each comprised of 2 Simulink elements.
Workaround: If the frame signal used in the Simulink model originates in another toolbox such as Vision HDL, you must transpose the matrix frame signal to adhere to the frame representation of HDL cosimulation. After transposing the frame signal, the signal type changes from frame-based to sample-based. To convert it back to a frame-based signal, you must use a Frame Conversion block, which can be found in the dspobslib library. See attached workaround image.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: coder.gpu.kernel pragma applies to incorrect loop
Description: When using the?coder.gpu.kernel ?pragma, the generated code might produce incorrect results if both  conditions are true: The pragma is applied to a loop that is either optimized away or automatically unrolled Another loop that is not safe to parallelize immediately follows or is contained in the previous loop  When these conditions are met, the code generator might convert the second loop to a GPU kernel. Because the second loop is not parallelizable, this transformation leads to the generation of incorrect code, resulting in incorrect output. Note:  Parallelization safety checks are always bypassed when using the?coder.gpu.kernel ?pragma. For example, the code generated for the entryPoint  MATLAB function produces incorrect results. function t = entryPointA = coder.nullcopy(zeros(1000,1));t = 0;coder.gpu.kernel;for i = 1:10    A(i) = i*i;endfor i = 1:1000    t = i;endend

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Growing nested cell array element via subscripted assignment
Description: The generated code might produce incorrect results if your MATLAB code satisfies all  these conditions:?? Your MATLAB code creates a nested cell array whose underlying elements are numeric arrays. All these underlying numeric arrays have the same size. For example, in the following nested cell array x, all the underlying elements are double scalars:  x = {{1,2},{3,4}}?  A subsequent assignment grows the size of one of these underlying numeric arrays. For example:   x{1}{2} = [4 5 6]   Your code does not change the size of any of the other underlying numeric arrays. Your code does not index into the nested cell array using nonconstant indices.  This issue affects MATLAB Coder, MATLAB Function blocks or MATLAB System blocks in Simulink, and MATLAB functions in Stateflow.

Summary: Transmit or Receive error when simulating or compiling a model that includes J1939 blocks
Description: A J1939 transmit or receive error might occur when simulating or compiling a model that includes J1939 blocks. When a model is not set up to execute the J1939 Network Configuration block, J1939 Node Configuration block, and J1939 Transport Layer block in the correct sequence, J1939 transmit or receive errors might occur when simulating or compiling the model. Placing any of these blocks within a subsystem that alters their execution order also might produce errors, such as: J1939 Transmit: PGN Registration Error: 7   J1939 Transmit: PGN Registration Error: 17   J1939 Receive: PGN Registration Error: 7   J1939 Receive: PGN Registration Error: 17
Workaround: Ensure that the blocks operate in the correct sequence: the J1939 Network Configuration block first, followed by the J1939 Node Configuration block, and then the J1939 Transport Layer block.

Summary: HDL Coder optimizations may have limitations with certain masked subsystems
Description: When using at least one of the following optimizations on or inside a masked subsystem, the optimization may not work. Clock-rate pipelining Resource sharing Streaming Hierarchy flattening  The limitations occur when the masked subsystem contains at least one of the following blocks (list is not exhaustive): MATLAB Function block Stateflow Chart System Object block Multiply, Divide, or Reciprocal blocks with HDL property?Architecture  set to?ShiftAdd  Sqrt block with block property Method  set to?Newton-Raphson  HDL Optimized blocks DSP HDL and Wireless HDL blocks Bus Creator Enum type signals in the subsystem hierarchy Xilinx System Generator or DSP BA blocks
Workaround: Avoid mask usage on subsystems containing the listed blocks for regions of the model where optimizations are also requested.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Sample time hit checks may be incorrect with re-scheduled reference models
Description: In some cases, the TID arguments passed to the initialization of referenced models is made in the wrong order. This causes incorrect timing checks in the reference model code. This may result in any of the following: Incorrect simulation results in accelerated simulation modes Incorrect results from generated code Crashes when running generated code  This behavior happens when the reference model has a schedule which does not match the schedule of the top model, and when any of the following are true in the referenced model: It contains a block which accesses time It contains a block which uses special sample hit such as a rate transition It contains an S-Function block with multiple sample times

Summary: Unable to enter ^ and ~ characters in MATLAB on Linux systems when using non-QWERTY keyboard layouts
Description: When using a non-QWERTY keyboard on Linux systems, the ^  and ~  keys do not work as expected. When you press these keys, the characters do not display on the screen and are not recognized by MATLAB. This problem affects most tools that contain editable text fields in the MATLAB desktop, including the Command Window, Editor, Live Editor, Current Folder Toolbar, Find Files, and Variables Editor. This problem also affects tools in the New Desktop for MATLAB Beta. MATLAB Online is unaffected and works as expected.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated code contains uninitialized local variable for AUTOSAR Classic model containing a function-call subsystem connected to root-level outport
Description: When generating code for an AUTOSAR Classic model that contains a root-level outport connected to a function-call subsystem, the generated code might contain an RTE write call that uses an uninitialized temporary variable. The generated code might contain explicit RTE write calls that reference uninitialized variables if all the following conditions are true: The model contains a function-call subsystem. The trigger for the function-call subsystem originates from any block other than an Inport  block at the root level of the model configured to output a function call. The function-call subsystem outputs to at least one Outport block at the model root level.?

Summary: A MATLAB System block with nested bus inputs in a referenced model might experience code generation assertion
Description: A MATLAB System block might experience an assertion during code generation when all of the following are true:? The MATLAB System block receives a nested bus input. The MATLAB System block is used in a model reference. The Simulation mode ?parameter of the model reference is set to Accelerator.

Summary: Obfuscatedfiles within the deployable archive (.ctf file) in fopen and MATLAB compilerworkflows
Description: To open anobfuscated file, specify the file path using the which  function, including whencalling fopen . By default, the -s flag obfuscates?.m , .mlapp , .p , v7.3 .mat , .mlx , .sfx , and MEX files. Tomanually include additional file types (such as .txt ) in the user package, addeach file type in a separate extension tag to the filematlabroot /toolbox/compiler/advanced_package_supported_files.xml .
Workaround: You can resolve this issue by first calling which and passing its output to fopen.function myfunc()    filename = which('test.mat');    fid = fopen(filename)endCompiling this version of myfunc via?mcc -s -m myfunc.m works as expected.

Summary: File log data imported to the Simulation Data Inspector might be missing
Description: File log data from a Simulink Real-Time model is sporadically missing when the data is imported into the Simulation Data Inspector while the Simulation Data Inspector Window is open.
Workaround: Close the Simulation Data Inspector before importing file log data. After the file has been successfully imported, you can reopen the Simulation Data Inspector.??
Fixed In: R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Incorrect array indexing in a for-loop for discontiguous signals
Description: The generated code for a Simulink block or S-function might use an incorrect for -loop index instead of producing an error when all of the following conditions are true: The block has a discontiguous input signal. The block has a tunable parameter that uses a field of a structure variable.
Workaround: Pass the block input signal through a Signal Conversion block that has the?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization parameter selected.If you are using an S-function with custom TLC code and Simulink R2023b or later, use the enhanced TLC interface.
Fixed In: R2022a Update 8, R2022b Update 8, R2023a Update 6, R2023b Update 5, R2024a

Summary: sum and prod misinterpret empty array of operating dimensions
Description: You can specify a vector of operating dimensions for the  sum  and  prod  functions.  If the size of the vector is 1x0 or 0x1, in releases affected by this bug sum  and prod  operate along the default operating dimension. However, the expected behavior is that sum  and prod  should not operate along any dimensions and should return an array of the same size as the input array. This is likely to cause problems when the operating dimension is programmatically generated, for example  sum(A,1:d)  or  sum(A,userProvidedSet) . Other functions that accept a vector of operating dimensions, such as  mean ,  max ,  all , and  any , error if the vector of operating dimensions is empty.  Example  >> A = magic(4)  A =??? 16???? 2???? 3??? 13???? 5??? 11??? 10???? 8???? 9???? 7???? 6??? 12???? 4??? 14??? 15???? 1  % Bug:>> sum(A, 1:0)ans =??? 34??? 34??? 34??? 34   % Correct behavior:>> sum(A, 1:0)ans = ??? 16???? 2???? 3??? 13???? 5??? 11??? 10???? 8???? 9???? 7???? 6??? 12???? 4??? 14??? 15???? 1
Workaround: Instead of calling?sum(A,dim)?where dim may be empty, call?sum(A,[dim ndims(A)+1]).Summing along the trailing singleton dimension of A has no effect, but avoids the special-case treatment for an empty vector of operating dimensions.Example>> A = magic(4)A =??? 16???? 2???? 3??? 13???? 5??? 11??? 10???? 8???? 9???? 7???? 6??? 12???? 4??? 14??? 15???? 1% Bug:>> sum(A,1:0)ans =??? 34??? 34??? 34??? 34 % Workaround:>> sum(A,[1:0 ndims(A)+1])ans =??? 16???? 2???? 3??? 13???? 5??? 11??? 10???? 8???? 9???? 7???? 6??? 12???? 4??? 14??? 15???? 1

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Persistent System objects incorrectly initialized within parfor loops
Description: If a persistent variable contains a System object, the code generator might produce code that executes the object's constructor and setupImpl  method at program initialization. If this persistent variable is accessed from the body of a parfor  loop, this initialization might not execute correctly. As a result, the System object's properties might be left in an uninitialized state causing incorrect results when they are accessed. Additionally, race conditions might occur when writing to the properties of the  object within the parfor  loop, causing nondeterministic behavior. This is an example of a System object that might cause this issue: classdef MySystemObj < matlab.System    properties        Prop    end     methods(Access=protected)        function setupImpl(obj, ~)            obj.Prop = 1;        end         function out = stepImpl(obj, ~)            out = obj.Prop;        end    endend In the generated code, accessing the property Prop ?might produce incorrect results. Also, modifying this property might incur race conditions during execution of the generated code.  A similar issue might also occur if Prop  were initialized in the constructor instead of in the setupImpl  method of MySystemObj .
Workaround: Introduce a coder.ceval call in the setupImpl method or the constructor of the System object. For example, modify the definition of MySystemObj as follows:classdef MySystemObj < matlab.System    properties        Prop    end    methods(Access=protected)        function setupImpl(obj, ~)            obj.Prop = 1;            coder.ceval('(void)', coder.ref(obj.Prop));        end        function out = stepImpl(obj, ~)            out = obj.Prop;        end    endendThe coder.ceval?call?introduces the following code into the generated setupImpl method:(void)(&obj.Prop);This code is ignored by the C compiler, but it forces the code generator to execute the setupImpl method after program initialization. This prevents the issue from occurring.

Summary: Coverage not reported in fast restart mode for logic implemented as part of model initialization
Description: Simulink Coverage incorrectly fails to measure coverage of logic when both of these conditions are true: The logic is implemented as part of model-initialization, for example, the logic is inside an Initialization subsystem. Fast-restart mode is enabled and the model is paused at the top of the simulation in the "Ready (Fast Restart)" state.

Summary: Setting up connection to Arduino board over Bluetooth or WiFi stalls on Mac OS 12 (Monterey) and higher versions
Description: After you install the MATLAB Support Package for Arduino Hardware on Mac OS 12 (Monterey) and higher versions, setting up a connection to the Arduino board over Bluetooth or Wi-Fi stalls in both cases - either while creating the arduino  object by specifying the Bluetooth address or IP address, or after choosing the connection type as Bluetooth or Wi-Fi in the Hardware Setup screen.

Summary: Polyspace compilation error when using Tasking compiler attribute?__align()
Description: If you run a Polyspace Bug Finder analysis by specifying compiler option -compiler tasking  and your code contains Tasking compiler attribute?__align() , you might get a compilation error.   For instance, if you run an analysis on this code:  ? #define STATIC_ASSERT(expr) extern char __static_assert[(expr) ? 1 : -1]? unsigned char __align(8) myvar;  ? typedef struct {? ?? unsigned int ui32d0;? ?? unsigned char ui8d1;? ?? unsigned char ui8d2;?? } mydummystr;  ? ?STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(mydummystr)==6);  You get an error message similar to this: ?? Verifying myfile.c (1/1)  ?? F:\pathto\myfile.c, line 11: error: the size of an array must be greater than zero??? |? extern char __static_assert[(sizeof(mydummystr)==6) ? 1 : -1];??? | ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ? ^  In this instance, Polyspace handles the Tasking attribute __align(8)  correctly, b ut?calculates the size of structures incorrectly afterward.
Workaround: Add option?-D __align(a)=?to the Polyspace analysis options and restart the analysis. Polyspace ignores the __align() attribute and calculates the structure size correctly.

Summary: Callback function specified by configureCallback?is sometimes?not executed
Description: A callback function specified as an input to the configureCallback ?method of the following?interface objects might not get triggered even when data is available to trigger it:serialport  tcpserver  tcpclient  udpport  bluetooth
Workaround: Use a MATLAB timer callback instead to implement similar behavior. For more information, see?timer.
Products: Industrial Communication Toolbox, Instrument Control Toolbox

Summary: RoadRunner fails to load Point Cloud files when a comma decimal point number locale is used
Description: During the process of preparing data to send to the PDAL library for loading and processing point clouds, RoadRunner encountered inconsistencies with some string conversions not aligning with PDAL's expectations. As a result, PDAL fails to parse the strings during Point Cloud load and prints an error saying "Invalid value for argument 'bounds'" in the output panel. This issue is more prominent on Linux.
Workaround: Switch number locale to period based decimal point (example: US English number locale) instead of a comma decimal point locale (example: German number locale).

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Code generated for Bitwise OR operation between negative integer and Boolean?variable might produce incorrect answers
Description: The code generator might produce incorrect answers if all the following conditions are true: The Casting modes  model configuration parameter is set to Standards Compliant . The Bitwise OR  operation is performed between a negative integer and a Boolean variable in the MATLAB code, for example:  bitor(-(int16(11), A, 'int16'); In the generated code, the negative integer is converted to an unsigned short type before performing the Bitwise OR  operation, which is incorrect.
Workaround: Replace signed integer type with unsigned integer type in the MATLAB code, for example:bitor(-(uint16(11), A, 'uint16');

Summary: HDL code generation fails with incorrect error when HDL FIFO block is present inside a Resettable subsystem
Description: HDL code generation throws incorrect error when your model has HDL FIFO block placed inside the Resettable Subsystem. The error message points to incorrect block. HDL code generation does not support FIFO block inside Resettable Subsystem.  The following error message is generated: A transform or optimization has created RAM block '<Path to incorrect block>' inside a resettable subsystem. This is not supported.?

Summary: Using a single sparse preconditioner for sparse iterative solvers on a GPU runs significantly slower when compared to previous releases
Description: Using a single sparse preconditioner M  for sparse iterative sovlers (bicg ,bicgstab , cgs , gmres , lsqr , pcg , qmr , and tfqmr  ) on a GPU runs significantly slower when compared to previous releases. The slowdown is more noticeable for sparse inputs with large numbers of nonzero elements and can be up to 10 times slower.

Summary: There is a simulation mismatch between the Simulink output and the HDL code output for the Delay block containing Enable and Reset ports whose Delay length Source is set to 'Dialog'.
Description: When the Delay block whose Delay length?Source? is set to 'Dialog'?has both enable and reset ports, there is a simulation mismatch between the output from Simulink and the HDL code. When the falling edge of the reset signal overlaps with the rising edge of the enable signal, the simulation mismatch is observed.
Workaround: The State control block?with 'Synchronous' mode can be added with Delay block whose Delay length?Source?is set to 'Dialog'?containing enable and reset ports. This mode ensures there is no simulation mismatch.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Generated AUTOSAR C code uses uninitialized local variables for explicit writes
Description: An AUTOSAR model generates RTE write function calls that use uninitialized local variables when all of the following conditions are true: The root outports are configured for AUTOSAR data access mode?ExplicitSend . The root outports are connected to an S-function that writes conditionally or partially to its output.?  Under these conditions, the generated initialization code for the model contains uninitialized stack variables, which RTE write function incorrectly calls to write to root outports.
Workaround: Insert Signal Conversion blocks between the S-function and root outports. In each Signal Conversion block, set the block parameter?Output?to?Signal copy?(the default) and select the option?Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Accessibility Issue: Toolstrip items lose focus unexpectedly in MATLAB Online
Description: In MATLAB Online, certain toolstrip items lose focus unexpectedly when they are accessed using the keyboard. For example: Closing or dismissing the drop-down menu of a button in the toolstrip does not bring focus back to the button in the toolstrip. Instead, the focus moves to the previously focused tool (such as the Editor, Command Window, Current Folder browser, or Workspace browser). Selecting or clearing the Show Code ?checkbox in the Figure Toolstrip Tab does not keep focus on the checkbox but instead moves focus to the previously focused tool.
Workaround: Use the Ctrl+F6 (Command+F6 on macOS) shortcut to move focus back to the toolstrip.For our best accessibility support, use?MATLAB Online. MathWorks is committed to improving the accessibility of its software products, focusing first on the accessibility of MATLAB Online. See the?Accessibility Statement for MATLAB?site for the latest information on accessible workflow support.
